contents: Re: On vacation Thank you all. I don't know why I'm not getting better. I feel like I've tried everything that's been suggested. I've been feeling like this for weeks now. I keep on taking pyridium in hopes that it will do something to help and it feels like there's no change. I took elavil at 10 and now it's 2 am and I've gone to the bathroom so many times and I can't sleep. I'm so miserable and I can't believe this is happening to me now - I am so lucky to be in Italy and I feel like the trip is ruined. I wish there was something I could get at the pharmacy to help. Thank you all for your support.
Re: On vacation Oh and I just realized I posted this in the wrong forum. I'm so sorry about that!

Re: On vacation Hi Nancy, I'm not sure if you are still in Italy, but I wanted to let you know that my husband and I planned a 20 year anniversary trip to Italy, Greece and Turkey in May of last year. We had planned it for two years! I ended up getting a flare about a month before we left. I was SO upset and it wouldn't go away. I ended up going anyway. I was taking vesicare at the time, which helped a tiny bit. Whenever you order anything tomato based in Italy, you can ask for it in white sauce. I know it seems hard to eat in Italy, but I managed and it was still good (fresh mozzarella, etc). I was so scared to drink the water in Italy, but found out that Italy has one of the safest drinking waters in the entire world (although everyone is different, so be careful). I did end up drinking the water and it didn't bother me. It was a long vacation (14 days including a cruise), but I survived. I was fortunate in that I had some really good days in with the bad. I would hate to have you miss the Colosseum or the Vatican...maybe Donna's suggestion of getting a wheelchair would work. I guess it depends if sitting bothers you more/less than walking. And I know you already know this, but try, try, try not to overstress about it. You are in Italy and you're in an IC flare. You can't change that, so try your best to relax and take it easy. If it means missing out on some sightseeing to relax a bit in your room, then that's fine. You do what you have to do to get through it. I was also wondering if you might be able to contact your doctor and get the ok to increase your elavil a bit. I don't know if you've ever tried a little bit higher dosage (like 20mg). I noticed a bigger improvement on 20, rather than 10 when I was flaring badly. I am so sorry you're feeling bad on such an amazing vacation, but you can get through this. Could you take a hot bath every night? That always helps me relax. Hoping you're doing better today, Jane
signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: On vacation Dear Jane, Thank you so much for your kind and thoughtful message. I appreciated it so much when I received it. This is the first chance I've been able to sit down at a computer to type a response. Thank you for your story. It is so awful that we both had flares during our long-awaited trips. I'm glad that you were able to have a few good days. I got back from Italy on Saturday night. I am so glad that I was able to go there!! I was miserable for most of it but I was feeling better for the last four days of the trip. During the first bout, I was so sad because traveling was something I used to love. I wanted my life to be full of travel - I wanted to see everything and live everywhere. I felt like this disease has taken away everything I loved and that my life was completely over. And I just turned 26. When I'm in a flare, I feel like it will last forever. It's so hard to tell what will make it stop. I worry that it'll last for months or years. I guess this one did last one month! I'm not all better but I do feel better than I was. When I was in the worst of it, I was taking Prelief all the time, Bladder Ease supplements, Pyridium, baking soda and water, Amitiptyline and medication to sleep (sometimes Ambien, sometimes Unisom). Nothing seemed to help. I don't know if one of these medications eventually started kicking in and working or if it was something different I did. What I did differently was: -stop straining, no matter what, even if I felt like my bladder wasn't empty -try to stick to the IC diet as much as possible in Italy - before, I was eating white pasta like spaghetti carbonara that I thought was safe but I just started eating vegetables because I thought that maybe something in the pasta was bad -drinking tons of water -I found a medication at an Italian pharmacy that may have helped tremendously (or it may have been something else, it's so hard to know!!) called Cistalgan. It's a combination of Flavoxate hydrochloride and propyphenazone. It looks like the only similar thing we have in the US is Urispas, which is just the Flavoxate. I wonder if taking Urispas would help me? I bought another 30-pill package of Cistalgan before I left but I don't think I'll ever be able to get it again. I am still having frequency but it's nothing like what it was. I just wonder what helped me so much - it's so hard to tell when you're taking everything possible to deal with these symptoms. What was the thing that worked? I kind of think it was the Cistalgan because I started feeling better after a day of taking it. But I'm not sure. Anyway, I'm home now and hopefully things won't feel so hopeless now that I can use the bathroom whenever I want. There was this one awful day in Italy where I went on a tour and the bus didn't have a bathroom, so every stop we made I spent trying to find a bathroom and waiting in line instead of seeing the city at all. Thank you so much for all your support. Your message made me smile and it's just good to know that I'm not alone. I have another appointment with my urologist on Friday and I think we're going to finally schedule a cystoscopy. My first urologist didn't even suggest that and I started seeing her 3 years ago. My sincere thanks!

My attitude towards the flare helps such as not dwelling on the symptoms and what they feel like. I tell myself it is a sensation. I do the same thing with anxiety and say I have had this before and nothing is permanent. Easier said than done. I use heating pad and since my pelvic floor and back are often involved I go to bed and listen to some relaxing music which also helps me and prayer is a help as well. I hope my words are helpful and may everyone here feel better.

contents: Re: This Helps Me It was hard sometimes to stay positive and it was easy to feel frustrated but I used to say to myself during the 50th flare - "This too shall pass", "This will eventually subside, it always does", and "I made it through these flares before, and I can do it again." It was a good reminder Songbird to pay attention to our self talk during a painful time and to try and take care of our emotional well being along with the physical.

It can be very hard at times to stay hopeful when we are in pain, but there is always tomorrow and that might be a better day. Warm hugs, Donna P.S. I've been reading your poems; you have a real talent. Donna and others thank you so much. I am honored to be helpful and glad you like my poetry. Yesterday excruciating pain, today feeling ok Thank you everyone.

However what I say to myself and not describe the sensations and stick with those thoughts can make a big difference. Much caring to all of you.

Does the desert harvest take long to see results? Can take 3 months. Other aloes are processed with citric acid.

Hi everyone! It has been months since I have been on here but there is good reason. I have felt GREAT! Better than I have since I was 15 years old! I just wanted to pass on my new coping strategy. I know it won't work for everyone and some of you have probably tried it but I wanted to share it anyway. I have been seeing a chiropractor who specializes in pain management, trigger point therapy and deep tissue work. He has a physical therapist on his staff as well. I have gone 3 days a week for 3 months with adjustments, muscle stim and physical therapy. I now am taking only 1 medication regularly (down from 13) and have been able to start exercising and have lost 16 pounds. If anyone wants specific info about this very specialized form of chiropractic feel free to pm me. They see me anytime of the day or night if I am flaring and really help me to work through the pain when I have it. It has been a miracle for me! :woohoo:

signature: Kim Diagnosed August 2001 Current IC meds: Elmiron (since 2001), Levaquin (one pill after intercourse to prevent UTIs), Effexor (for depression & anxiety) Past IC meds: Amitriptyline (Elavil), Hydroxyzine (Vistaril), Detrol LA, Lexapro (for depression & anxiety, but also helped my IC) (They all helped, but I was able to discontinue them.) I've been virtually symptom free and able to eat & drink whatever I'd like for about 8 years now. ***************************** “We who lived in concentration camps can remember the men who walked through the huts comforting others, giving away their last piece of bread. They may have been few in number, but they offer sufficient proof that everything can be taken from a man but one thing: the last of the human freedoms -- to choose one's attitude in any given set of circumstances, to choose one's own way.” ~ Viktor Frankl “You cannot control what happens to you, but you can control your attitude toward what happens to you, and in that, you will be mastering change rather than allowing it to master you.” ~ Brian Tracy

contents: Thank you for sharing this, I'm so glad you've found something that helps! Is there any way we could tempt you to talk about your tip here? Is it something that can be done by yourself at home, or do you need to go to a Chiropractor to have it done? Just curious... Blessings, Lori
Wonderful news! I am so happy that you shared your good new with us.

I have improved 95% since doing Physical Therapy! I think Everyone diagnosed with I.C. should try physical therapy, BUT make sure you are seeing a therapist who has been trained in womens health issues, I CAN NOT STRESS THAT ENOUGH! All P.T.s are not created equal. I have been going off and on for about six months and I have improved 95%. Before I started P.T. I was like so many of you, feeling like I have to "go" about every twenty minutes and getting up several times every night. What a nightmare! I am mostly having normal days again, I am drinking and eating anything I want again and I am very thrilled with my outcome. I have cut back on exercising and am finding that the less I do the better I feel, so I have decided to give my pelvic floor muscles a rest for about 3 months then I will SLOWLY get back into my work outs.

I also feel better after beginning PT; but it's true, the ideal situation is to be referred to a physcial therapist who specializes in pelvic pain and pelvic floor dysfunction. Not all physical therapists know what to do with the pelvis, unfortunately. I'm happy to hear people are finding relief from their symptoms here... that is just wonderful
signature: **** Jen *Diagnosed with severe IC in 2004 *Also diagnosed with PFD, fibromyalgia, chronic myofascial pain, IBS, migraines, allergies/asthma, dermatographism *Kept trying a million different treatments for all these things until I found what works, and I am doing okay these days with the help of a cocktail of medications and the InterStim, which was first placed in 2007. [I have had 2 revisions - one in 2010 when my battery died and had to be replaced, and one complete replacement (lead and generator) in 2012 after a fall on my stairs caused my lead to move.] *Current meds include Atarax (50mg at night), Lyrica (150mg twice a day), Xanax (0.5mg at night and as needed), Zanaflex (4mg at night), hydrocodone (10/325, every 6 hours as needed), Advair, Nasonex, Singulair (10mg at night), oral contraceptives, home instills containing Elmiron and Marcaine (as often as I need to do them). **I am not a medical authority nor do I offer definitive medical advice. signature: Discover yourself like a lotus flower in full bloom, even in a muddy pond, beautiful and strong.......

contents: Re: Urethra Burning Originally posted by nathangela I am wondering if anyone has any tips of what you do for Urethra Burning? Thank You for any suggestions If you think there's any possibility it's externally caused, I do have some suggestions: (1) Be sure to use plain white, unscented, toilet tissue and don't wipe --- blot. (2) Wear only underpants with 100% cotton crotches and double rinse when you launder them. (3) Don't put soap on the tender skin around your urethra & vagina. (4) Try one of the spray bottles available in the ICN Shop to rinse after you urinate so there's no urine residue. (5) Shower instead of tub bathing sometimes helps. Donna

contents: Re: Urethra Burning Hello again! I never knew about the bottle to use...great idea. I'm in all cotton and only use seventh generation scent free detergent. I do wonder about my liners-I use Poise-they have a design on them and I wonder if they are made with chlorine and maybe some other chemicals that are causing irritation. It's inside as well. This did not start until my urologist did the test to see how much urine my bladder could hold . They inserted a tiny one in the urethra-it hurt quite a bit-actually my husband and I noticed that after that test my symptoms became worse. I will give what you have suggested a try. Thank You for your help! Angela

contents: Re: Urethra Burning I get it too, especially around my monthly. signature: Struggling with urethral pain for 19 years. When it started in 95 I was told this was psychosomatic. I coped quite well with lots of remissions. Just when I got nervous and was stressed I had urethral pain and felt like I had to pee all the time. Cysto/hydro with biopsies in 2012, result: no IC. Had a first, second and third opinion and the result was always PFD or chronic pelvic pain syndrome. I encourage everyone with these symptoms to get a proper diagnosis! I am doing physiotherapy based on yoga exercises, I had been pain free for almost a year... Now unfortunately it is back. So I am still struggeling Greetings from Denmark!

contents: Re: Urethra Burning Ditto on the ice. Either an ice cube or a frozen water bottle. It seems to help me by quieting the inflammation and spasms, numbing the area, and serving as a "counter irritant."

contents: So I used to use baby wipes when I peed. Then I started getting yeast infections. So I switched back to toilet paper. But the toilet paper started cutting at my skin, wiping too much I guess. So my question is what do you use? My lips are bleeding right now from the toilet paper and the wipes are burning them. Oh the joys of peeing way too much!

Originally posted by Boonky So I used to use baby wipes when I peed. Then I started getting yeast infections. So I switched back to toilet paper. But the toilet paper started cutting at my skin, wiping too much I guess. So my question is what do you use? My lips are bleeding right now from the toilet paper and the wipes are burning them. Oh the joys of peeing way too much! I only use plain white, unscented toilet tissue --- and don't wipe --- blot! Just hold the tissue there for a few seconds to absorb any leftover moisture. Since you've already got some irritation there, you might want to get one of the perineal wash bottles available in the ICN Shop to rinse away any urine. You could also try something as simple as Vaseline on the area to protect from your urine. If you're not already, I suggest you switch to underpants with cotton crotches and double rinse to be sure there isn't any soap residue left. Also, if you're washing your perineal area with soap --- don't. I hope this goes away soon. I know you're miserable. Donna

contents: Re: Toilet paper vs wipes dont know if you can get it in the US but i ad canisten hygene rinse to all my undies and towels in the wash. Like Donna said blot dont wipe then if at home wet a clean face washer (one you keep just for this purpose) and using nothing but water wipe the area very gently to remove urine then pat dry. contents: As a side note, last Wednesday when I was meeting with my GI doctor, I was bragging that I hadn't had an IC flare in 2-3 months. Since we are trying to get on top of my IBS symptoms, he recommended an abdominal ultrasound with a full bladder and gave me a prescription for pancreatic enzymes (pancrelipase) which i started taking on Thursday night. I had the ultrasound on Friday and took two doses of the enzymes and by Friday afternoon I was in a rip roaring flare. So what caused the flare? Could it have been the ultrasound or the enzymes? I haven't taken another dose of the enzymes just in case. Any ideas?

Hi everyone, Just wanted to share something with you that my urologist told me the other day. It kind of blew my mind. You can take it or leave it, but I choose to take it. He said that only about 5% of people diagnosed with IC actually have it. He told me that IC is the "wild west" of bladder/urinary problems. He said that so many urologists and doctors diagnose patients with IC too quickly, and that's why the treatments don't always work. It could be because you don't actually have IC. He explained to me that lots of people come to him already believing that they have it, but there's actually something else going on. He said there could be dozens of other things going on. He said, "IC is a real thing, but lots of urologists lean on it when they can't find the real problem." He said that the installations can actually harm the bladder of a patient who doesn't really have IC. He said that there are too many different symptoms among IC sufferers for it to be all IC. This was a massive relief to me. MASSIVE. I fully expected to leave his office diagnosed with IC. But instead, he spent the time ruling out things, checking me, looking at my urine, and talking to me about the complexities of our bodies. He said he will get to the bottom of my issues, but he isn't going to jump right into, "Oh, you have interstitial cystitis." He's very smart and frankly, I was surprised at his counsel. But very relieved. If you've been diagnosed with IC and your treatments aren't working, I don't think it would hurt to try another urologist. You might not actually have it!

contents: I'm sure there might be some people out there who have been misdiagnosed. However, I was diagnosed by hydrodistention with biopsies so there's no doubt. I hope you don't have IC, but if you've had these symptoms for several months I hope this doctor is willing to work with you towards a diagnosis. Mine did and I have confidence in what he told me. I do doubt the 5% quotation. Many physicians are reluctant to make an IC diagnosis --- and in at least some instances, it is actually underdiagnosed. Donna

contents: I worry it is under-diagnosed. I'm not up on everything current but I thought I read somewhere that even in a patient without visible IC in the bladder, that IC could still be the issue. I had everything else ruled out in 2000 but continued to have problems. It was only when my symptoms got VERY bad in 2007 that they looked inside and my dr. said yes it was IC. I took those same pics to two other urologists who told me my bladder looked fine. 3 years later I was told I had red patches and inflammation. That uro didn't think much of my first bladder pics either. I don't really know except that I have all the symptoms and I'm very diet sensitive.
signature: The combo of things that brought me out of remission were artificial sweetners, liquid vitamins with citric acid, tight pants, lower body workouts and stopping all my allergy meds cold turkey during height of allergy season. I wish I knew back then what I know now about IC First IC symptoms Spring of 2000; In-office cystoscopy 2002 -negative; Remission until 2007 (yeah!!); Negative PST spring of 2007; IC diagnosed via Cystoscopy and Hydrodistention 7/24/07; Another In-office cystoscopy 4/9/10 with biopsy showed inflammation Treatments for my IC/PFD: Elmiron 400 mg per day; walking, Zyrtec, Claritin, Singulair, Neurontin 100 mg per day; Ice packs and laying down; About to try Cytotec Tried and Failed: Heat (always makes me worse!), Heparin instills -too painful for my urethra; Atarax (interfered with my quality of sleep first time, second time dried my bladder out; Elavil makes me sick, lidocaine patches (just plain don't work), Cystoprotek (only tried a couple months but didn't seem to do much), Aloe Vera caps, pelvic floor therapy, trigger point work flared me worse, Lyrica (bad stomach pain) Other Diagnoses: PFD 2009; Fibromyalgia 2006, Osteopenia 2012, GERD 2006, Gastroparesis (delayed gastric emptying) Anxiety: Other medications: Ambien, Dexilant, Ibuprofren, Necon 1/35, Voltaren gel for tailbone BABY GIRL 10/28/08 *** BABY BOY 7/8/11

contents: I agree with Donna and Charisse. It took me over a year to get diagnosed, by way of a cystoscopy under general anaesthetic. The first doctor I saw missed it entirely, immediately assuming I had overactive bladder. The second one only did the test reluctantly. But he finally admitted I DID have IC, when the cystoscopy and biopsy showed bad inflammation and mast cells in my bladder lining. So when I read this post it really bemused me. I think the doctor is probably wrong about this!

Thanks for your input guys! I think it's all very interesting. I think that eventually, IC will be "broken up" into different conditions. Right now, it's a very blanket disease in my opinion. We all have such different symptoms and a treatment that may work for one patient might cause even further discomfort for another. To me, that is not the same disease. My urologist is attempting to search for the true causes. He diagnoses IC plenty, he's just careful about it and looks into other possibilities. He said that many of his patients come to him who have already been diagnosed with IC, but then he finds out what is truly wrong with them. One woman actually had a lodged kidney stone; another had urethral syndrome and not IC at all. A simple dose of Valtrex cured her of a 3-year pain. I am not saying that NO ONE has IC. I just think that if you've been diagnosed with it, and the treatments aren't working -- it may be something else. That's all. It's such a tricky, elusive condition that I think people with this type of pain should keep an open mind. If it IS interstitial cystitis, fine. But it may not be.

contents: All of my doctors told me that it is a diagnosis via exclusion of any and everything else that it could be. I went and saw a nurse practitioner who examined me and ran every test in the country. She sent me to doctor #1. He did the same and even had an ultrasound of my bladder too. He told me that I had IC. I did not believe him and went to find another doctor. I did not tell doctor #2 anything. I let him start at the beginning with only my self proclaimed symptoms. I had never heard of IC so I couldn't have been manifesting symptoms *after* reading about it. Dr. #2 said that I had IC. I didn't believe him so I saw Dr. #3. So far that's 1 NP, 1 OB/GYN, and 2 urogynocologists. But I didn't want to believe either of the uro-gyno's so I moved on to another one (Dr. #4) and again walked in cold turkey. This time I told him that I had been tested for everything, I had submitted to a biopsy (OUCH), I had been examined by a different uro-gyno. He repeated tests and scoped me and there I was with my "official" diagnosis. But wait, there's more. I went on to see Dr. #5 and all my attempts at denial ended there. I tried to wish it away. I tried to ignore it. I hoped "they" were wrong. BUT, one of my main points is this: Does a UTI responds to dietary changes? Does a STD respond to dietary changes? Does bladder cancer respond to dietary changes? Isn't that at least one way to know that our Doctors are not just throwing a false diagnosis at us because it's popular right now?

Originally posted by Alma LoriSue, I will definitely ask him what else it could be. While I was there, he literally listed about 9 things. What are your exact symptoms? Alma - my symptoms are varied and depending on the day, hour, minute, it changes. Sometimes it's just pressure, the feeling of having to urinate all the time whether my bladder is full or not. Then I can have a mild burning sensation with the pressure or by itself and can be accompanied by urethral pain/burning. There is no rhyme or reason to why/when these symptoms occur. They happen whether I eat or don't eat. The only thing I've noticed is that the end of the day the symptoms worsen. But I've read that's true with many of us. It may just be psychological that at the end of a long day when you're trying to ignore your symptoms, they seem more severe when you don't have work to occupy your time. I find it difficult to concentrate on anything at night. I can't read, watch TV...nothing keeps my mind from it. I think that it isn't that IC is being diagnosed incorrectly...I think IC (like my son's autism) is a huge umbrella and under it are many different symptoms and reasons for them. Not one person reacts like another which is why it's so hard to treat. I hope your doctor can find that magic "thing" we all pray for so your symptoms go away. As for me? I'll just keep throwing new things at it hoping to knock it off it's axis for a bit and give me some relief.

contents: LoriSue, your symptoms sound a bit like mine (but then, all of our symptoms are a little alike). I will ask him and see what he says. I hope that ALL of our symptoms go away! If I could, I would fix every single person's bladder on these forums. Clevsea, thanks for sharing that. I'm so sorry for your pain, but it's good that you got multiple opinions. That's so important. Honestly, getting multiple opinions was my only main point of this original thread. Again, as I said before, I do think that some of us (even myself, maybe) actually have IC. I don't think that every doctor is throwing a current disease at us. I just think that some of them are, and that some patients may be misdiagnosed. As with anything! This goes for other non-urinary conditions, too. I just brought it up here to encourage someone who may be suffering while getting treatment. I think -- and hope -- that in about 5-10 years, the blanket of IC will be separated into things like "summitate bladder cystitis" and "cicatrice cystitis" and things like that. So that we aren't all just pushed into the same boat, when we have such varying issues.

I agree Alma, some diagnoses wind up serving as umbrella terms or like the point Lori Sue made (which I agreed with 100%) there winds up being a continuum. There are lots of things on the Autism continuum, for example, and we all know that no 2 autistic people act exactly the same or have the same exact symptoms.

I agree Alma! IC may be very real, but its only because no one is getting to the source. Which for everyone is different. I finally went to an Immunology doctor and he found that I had Lyme disease with the co-infection of Mycoplasma. Plus I have Candida like crazy. I'm basically a walking Petri dish. I'm still waiting to see a Lyme doctor, but I'm currently on anti-fungals for the yeast. I'm treating the Lyme with natural antibiotics at the moment. I've felt better the last 2 days then I have in months. IC is real! But if you're not getting better, look elsewhere. And I say look elsewhere anyways! When the doctor tells you that you have something and they have no idea what causes it, then you have no choice but to do your own research. Good Luck to all of us!
Interesting, jjekblad. Being told you had Lyme disease must have been a big surprise. Good to hear you're feeling better!! I'm curious to know what your symptoms are/were?

Hello everyone, I'm in a flare right now and I'm also having pain in my lower left side. I don't know if it is IC or what? Does anyone else have this pain? I thought it might be my IBS but I had an appointment with my gastrologist yesterday and he didn't know what it was? He pushed on my sides and it hurt on both sides and in the middle by the time he was finished. I could use any suggestions. Thanks Linetta

Linetta, This may not be what you are experiencing, but I have had pains like that on both sides (mericfully they seem to alternate, I don't know what I would do if they came at the same time!) My pain is caused by ulcers in my bladder, they are called Hunn's or Hunts ulcers, I forget. Any how I feel like someone has got a hot knife going from my groin area and up and is twisting it. I can be fine one moment and then be on the floor the next. I definately sympathize, the pain can be indescribable. Good luck. Pamela Joy

contents: I had a hysterectomy due to endo, so I don't have ovaries. I wonder if it could be scar tissue? It is so hard to know what pain goes with what. I just get so tired of going to doctors. I spent almost the whole day in the doctors office on Wednesday just to be told they didn't know what the pain could be. Thanks for all the support!!!

I get that pain all the time, reason why I began seeking help in the first place, and I dont have endo nor any cyst on my overies. I also have severe pain in my lower back when I get up in the morning and as the day goes on it let's up but is always there. They suggested at first it was my tilt uterus however that has let up a bit since I had my cysto/hydro done 3 weeks ago and now I have it on occasion. My gyno was the first doc I went to for that pain and he did all the test needed to verify that (test were done in oct last month) So he sent me to the uro to see if I have ic. Well turns out I do and I still have that pain. It's a cramping pain constently and then at times it moves to both sides or in the middle or all three. So I would have to say that it's your ic.

contents: I suffered for a year with pain in the lower left side. Went to uro, gyno and every other "o" that would see me. Mine turned out to be Ulcerative Colitis. First, they treated me for IBS, but I continued to hurt. Did colonoscopy and found UC. Asacol has helped with the constant pain, but I still experience problems on a daily basis. The IC irritates the UC and vice versa. Good luck and don't give up - there is someone out there that will help us!

I'm pretty sure I can help here... I had that same pain in those same locations and I too have had a total hyster and the pains ended up coming from adhesions (scar tissue) that grew from previous pelvic surgeries - so I'm assuming that's what it is. A general surgeon fixed my problem a few years back and for some reason about every 3-5 years I have to go in and get "cleaned out" cause those adhesions come and grow with any abdominal surgery you might encounter. Try calling your gyn and see if he'd be willing to go in laprascopically and look for adhesions otherwise try your MD and see if he can refer you to a general surgeon he might highly recommend. It's same day surgery, they go in with the laproscope and it's three-four small puncture sights on your belly, usually one on the left, one on the right, a belly button incision and one right above your pelvic hair line. PM me if you have other questions. Sometimes my pain was enough to double me over and have been having this pain off and on lately. Let me know how you come out. I'm a concerned IC friend, okay? ~Tracy K. Sending you an angel to watch over you -
signature: Love from far away, Tracy K Current & Past Health Diagnosis(s) Currently suffering from 2nd Bladder Prolapse Interstitial Cystitis- March 2004 Fibromyalgia 2004 Chronic Fatigue Syndrome 2005 Severe Pelvic Floor Dysfunction Irritable Bowel Syndrome Tension & Migraine Headaches Chronic/Severe Urinary Retention Numerous Endometriosis/Adhesions Removal Surgeries Pelvic Organ Prolapses (Uterus/Vaginal Walls, Bladder, & Rectum) Partial Hysterectomy 1997 Ovaries Removed due to large cysts 1998 Chronic UTI's (ongoing) Medtronic Interstim Unit removed after 5 months due to severe infection and unit was floating around in my lower back 2004 Never be ashamed to ask for help when you need it. God will never give me more than I can handle, but sometimes I wonder if he lost track of what's he's dealt out here ???? I'm trying here

contents: Thanks everyone for the advice. I guess I'll call my gyno. I hate having lapro. again. I've had soo many test this year. I'm so afraid the endometriosis has came back. When I had only 1 ovary left it came back and my ovary and falliopian tube fused together. I couldn't hardly walk. It hurt so bad!!! Again thanks for all the support. Linetta

I was just diagnosed and that is what started my path of problems. First i was diagnosed with IBS. Then i still had the same pains in the lower left area. I had everything from MRI's to CAT SCANS, blood work and been violated in many areas. I hate the sound of latex gloves now. So finally after all the poking and proding, my uro doctor told me that i have IC. I know how you feel. I've been through 4 doctors before my diagnosis. I would also try your gyno or perhaps another opinion from a doctor. I wish you luck. I'm in the same boat.

I have pain mostly in my right side (where the appendix is) but the left side seems to like to have its voice in the pain-o-meter. I am glad to hear that pain from what seems to be in the intestinal area can also be a sign of IC. I have been told by docs that people with IC only have pain centered around the bladder, but my theory is when your bladdar is hurting, everything around it wants to hurt too. I hope you feel better.

Hmm Well, my experience says the doc who said that is quite wrong. Keeping the IC under control has pretty much reduced my pain and sure, it seemed to center around the bladder.... and the urethra.... and the colon.... and the genital area, even the surface regions hurt.... Myli

my pain is in my right side and its due to hunners not saying this is your problem but that is what is causing all my pain. maybe you have a hunner coming up on your bladder this can be taking care of soemtimes with just cocktail treatments of heparin other times they may use a dmso and if this doesnt work they can go in and take them off the bladder. Please call your doctor so they can find out what is going on. sending you hugs and prayers Rhonda

contents: Thanks for all the ideas. I finally made it to an IC support group last month that has a doctor that specializes in IC. The next meeting is in 2 weeks. I think I will talk to him. He knows so much more than my uro. I really need to switch but he is getting ready to retire. The pain isn't so bad now that my IC has calmed down, I think it has a lot to do with IC. Thanks Linetta

I get a crippling spasm, usually on the left side running down into my groin. It's so bad it makes me just stop and go, "Ok, that was bad." and I get scared to move again. It usually just comes and goes and I really don't know what causes it. When I had the exact same pains when I was pregnant (before IC) they admitted me for a week but never found the cause just said it must have been IBS. I only recently had a laparoscopy which showed nothing (no endo either) so I think it must be IC. It bloody hurts though! Sara :dogrun:
signature: Sara- 26 year old mum to Claudia (31.10.01- 4 going on 14!!) and baby Alexander (06.01.06). IC patient since horrible UTI 2003... "Animals are my friends... and I don't eat my friends."

contents: That was my first complaint. I started having pain in my lower left abdomen and pelvic area. Is your pain in the pelvic/groin area or is it in your abdomen? My pain is just above where my thigh meets the hip just left of the pubic synthesis. It is still my main and worst symptom. I had an ex-lap where they cut me open at my navel and looked at my uterus and ovaries to check for endometreiosis but it was clean. After that I had the cyst/hydro and was diagnosisied with IC. I do have frequency, on an average day I go to the bathroom 20-30 times a day, more if I am in a flare. I know how frustrating it is to be in so much pain and the doctors don't know why. Good Luck.

I remember a long time ago, long before IC, I had pain as you describe and it turned out to be Pelvic inflammatory disease. I got to the ER in time and took a long dose of antibiotics and everything was okay.

I piped in earlier on this thread, but let me further explain. I have the exact pain that Amanda explained. Had a lap and cysto/hydro (many other cysto/hydro's mingled in) at the same time. Then I had a flex sig that was extremely painful. Gastro diagnosed IBS, but my symptons continued to worsen to the point I had blood in my stool. That is when they did a colonoscopy and they diagnosed Ulcerative Colitis. My pain in the lower left side was my Sigmoid Colon reacting to UC. Since being on Asacol the pain has diminished to an acceptable level, but I still have bouts with long bathroom trips. When UC flairs so does IC! I am still working on a daily basis to learn to live with these diseases, but it helps somewhat to know the cause. Right now, I am anemic and my ANA level is sky high. My firm belief is that we are treating my symptons right now, and that eventually they will find the root cause in my immune system. Best of luck to everyone...

Hi Linette, I get my worse pain in my back either lower back pain or upper flank pain on both sides, most of the time it is on my right side. I am told that it is from IC and that it is those pain nerve things in your bladder that is sending out the pain and according each individual (everyone is different) is where the brain is registering the pain.... However, when it is really bad, I get tested for a possible bladder/kidney infection, because that cause back pain too, and then you will need an antibiotic. Good idea to give your Uro a call, especially if you have never had the back pain before. Good Luck!!
signature: Traci "If God brings you to it...He will bring you through it."

contents: I too have the pain in left side I have had the pain also in my left side. No one seems to be able to figure out the cause. It doesn't seem to be directly from the IC but may be related. The pain is always present - like a dull throbbing ache - and at times will become severe (a 10+ on a scale of 1-10). A few times a week I will be doubled over in pain to where I can't move. Sometimes this when I'm driving down the road. Also, at times the pain radiates down my entire left leg. My uro sent me for physical therapy which seemed to stir things up even more but she wants me to continue with therapy. She expained that things may get worse before they get better. The therapist may try lumbar traction this week - I'm nervous about this - what is that like? Has anyone had this done? At the same time I'm also willing to try anything to make the pain go away. I also had a hysterectomy and removal of my left ovary. At the time of the hysterectomy they found my left ovary quite tangled up with my bowel and the pelvic well. They were able to untanlge it and remove it - I thought WOW - that should take care of my pain! But, oh now, still have the pain and it has progressively gotten worse. Now I'm interested to hear if it is more directly related to the IC.

contents: I originally was checked for a fractured rib because I had gotten into a car accident then they checked my colon and my kidneys and everything and can't find a reason for my pain. I've done 30+ pregnancy tests and I'm 22 and have been diagnosed with IC since may this year. The pain is still unexplained. I still have UTIs and kidney infections and once lost bladder control. My bladder is hyperactive and to large for my body (I don't have to go for 2days before itll be full) and yet I still have this pain& it got worse with my hydrodistention two days ago. Has anyone else noticed its itchy though?

I wasn't really sure where to post this question but can anyone give me some advice of stuff to wear when having a flair? I've heard baggy cloths and cotton underwear. Does anyone have any advice for what kind of cloths I should keep on the look out for? I'm trying to find decent sweat pants but if anyone could give me some advice for jeans i'd greatly appreciate it!

contents: I wear maternity boot cut jeans with a long shirt. The best ones have a small band instead of a full belly band. No matter what size you are it would be best to try them on before buying. Around the house I wear PJ bottoms or sweat pants. I hope this helps you. Hug, Ziggy
Loose skirts
signature: 27 year old student Had symptoms since I was 5 Been on forums since '07, knew I had this since then, received mild treatment without true diagnosis due to anxiety. Diagnosed by symptoms on 4/08/11. Had first horrible flare in 07, bad doctors, had to research IC myself to clue in onto what it was. After following the diet I went into remission. Big flare in 09 due to an untreated bladder infection. Went back into remission in 2012. Symptoms returned 4/1/15. All of my remissions have ended in April-May or September-October. It sounds like a connection to seasonal allergies to me.. Currently: Continuous Birth Control, vaginal valium (10/6/11), Elmiron (4/08/11), UTA (4/08/11), continuous Trimethoprim for infections (10/6/11), Heating pad, Benedryl, harsh diet What I know works: Benedryl, diet, birth control, UTA, valium, ditropan, soma What didn't work: Elavil, vistaril (12/22)

contents: I wear my jeans (Westport from Dress Barn, they're stretchy & the waistband hits just right) a size big so they don't bind. I couldn't wear jeans at all though, my first 2 years with IC. Then I wore long skirts with sandals in summer and knee-high boots in winter. I made sure they were long enough I didn't need tights or pantyhose (still can't wear those). I do live in California, though, so I don't have super-cold weather to deal with. I always tried to be a little fashionable, hoop earrings, makeup, so I didn't feel too frumpy. I was a Spanish teacher then and when we were describing clothing in Spanish, I heard one of the kids saying, "That's her style (with the long skirts and boots)" I was glad to hear I had a style, especially from teens who can be a bit judgmental sometimes It was a bright spot in a crummy year! (and told me it was worth the effort to try to make the best of the modifications then). I am happy to say though, that with treatment, diet, and gentle exercise, I am able to wear my favorite jeans, sweaters and boots again! That really is more my style. Just throwing that in there to say that what you do today to manage IC, may not be the same when your treatment plan works! (My IC is not mild, the nurse at Stanford actually described it as a very bad case. So there is hope out there.)

contents: It is so interesting to know that other people have bought maternity jeans. I bought them in the past, too. I just found some jeans called Jag pull on jeans that are really cute and stylish. They have a wide flat elastic waistband. I bought them at a local store in town but found them on the Internet, too. Here's the website. Since I have had a flare the last few weeks, I have been wearing mainly dresses. When I am at home, I wear my robe only. Now that it is getting cooler, I will wear leggings with long shirts. I have always cut the leggings around the waistband for more comfort. Nobody ever sees because of the long shirts I wear with them. Hope this helps some.

I wear Karen Scott knit (jersey fabric) slacks. I get them at Macy's....they are available in both petite and regular sizes. I love jersey fabric since they never feel tight over the bladder. I can't stand anything tight near the bladder.

I just bought me some Danskin now relaxed fit pants at Wal-mart the other day. They we're only 11-12 dollars, they were folded up on a shelf area, they fit great and are really comfortable. They had them in black, dark blue and grey. I got a pair of each.

If I'm flaring at work - loose skirts are my best friend along with cotton underwear. I also wear cotton leggings with a flat waistband (I buy a size larger so there's room in the waist area) and a long shirt. You can also get pants that have stretch in them -- In Canada we have a store called Reitman's and they sell cloths that are called "comfort fit" At home - pyjama pants or yoga pants, loose tops and I usually skip the underwear if I can.
signature: You must be the change you want to see in the world. ~ M.K. Ghandi

contents: I am so glad that somebody started this thread! I have been trying to come up with more creative ideas for what I can wear. Right now I am very limited because I cannot stand anything that puts pressure on my pelvis or abdomen. My go to items are one super loose and flowy skirt (though sometimes when I am flaring badly even that puts too much pressure) and a couple pairs of Danskin pants (I don't know what you would call them..yoga pants, lounge pants, workout pants?) that I bought two sizes too big. I just recently found a couple of cheap pairs of wal-mart brand pants (on sale for three dollars each!!) that I tried on just for the heck of it and found they actually fit really well in the legs but were very roomy in the waist. I really miss jeans, the idea of buying maternity jeans is a great idea, I will have to look into that! I love these boards, the more I read (listen) the more tips and treasures I find!!
signature: My life in a nutshell: So I and whenever I can. I spend a lot of time in and occasionally I spend a lot of time . This is my life and I take it one day at a time.

contents: Like others have said, I find that anything that is not too restrictive tight on the hips/pelvis helps to avoid added pressure. My biggest issue was finding underwear that I didn't feel restricted in. Full briefs really aggravated my hips ( i have tight hip/pelvic muscles) and felt like I couldn't 'breathe' down there. I didn't want to go without .. for the obvious reasons! Luckily, I found my miracle brand at Target; super silky stretchy lightweight thongs - feel's like i'm not wearing anything, plus they are pretty! They do have them in bikini style too. Not cotton, but I've never had a problem with the fabric. Target : Expect More. Pay Less. http://www.target.com/p/gilligan-o-malley-women-s-seamles-thong-assorted-colors-patterns/-/A-14055189#prodSlot=large_1_20
signature: IBS - Diagnosed in 8/2010, under control through probiotics IC - sudden onset 7/2012, diagnosed 9/2012 - Remission starting 3/13 - (Usually deal with 2-4 flares a year that last from a day to a week and then go away) 6/15 - Mega Flare Medications while I struggle with IC: Elavil 10mg (started 9/2012), Elmiron (started 8/2012), Hydroxine (As needed at night), Claritin , Junel FE (Birth Control) , Probiotic, Aloe Vera 6x a day, Pyridium, uribel (as needed) Fennel tea & Mint tea helps a lot! Yoga is a life saver! Starting Physical Therapy for pelvic floor spasms 10/12/12 - ended 12/12Streches are always useful! Lots of Water!

contents: I found some great ones at Wal-mart made by Danskin, they're very comfortable and they fit kind of like maternity pants around the belly yet they kind of look like dress pants. I bought the relaxed fit. I've wore cotton underwear for years so couldn't tell you if that makes any difference or not. I have also found that Kotex is actually making pads and liners now that don't have chlorine in them and have aloe in them. Found the a Walgreens haven't looked for them anywhere else yet. So glad I don't have to make a special trip to the health food store to pay $5+ for 18 pads and the liners were actually taped to the package of pads so one price for all and it is a big package of both.

I am going to describe my symptoms to see if this sounds like IC or just overactive bladder.. My symptoms are first a "pressure" in the bladder - pressing feeling, which i have never had before quite like this,, but it is similar to normal urgency with a real bite to it. It is sort of like the urge to urinate as though i have a gallon of h20 in there... then it gets so intense that it hurts. THe pain to me goes into the gential area,, which i suppose is the urethra.. I dont know! It is a pressure and burning at the same time. A raw " wet " feeling, but I am not leaking. When i urinate, I can still feel a raw, pulling, almost "ripping" feeling in the bladder, but not nearly as intense. 20 minutes later it gets intense and painful again. I am not letting my self pee any more often than 1 and 1/2 to 2 hours apart so my bladder does not shrink. Is that a good idea or not? These symptoms are the least between 5 am and 10:00 am. I start drinking water at 8, then do a smoothy at 9 and by 11:00 the symptoms are there again. What does that sound like to you all? thanks again.
signature: Surf Gal

contents: Everyone's symptoms will be different, mine are a "finger scratching the bladder feeling", a "gotta go" feeling and a burning in the urethra. Sometimes theres a pulling feeling too.

I wouldn't recommend not letting yourself urinate when you need to. At least my uro told me that isn't good to do, so I try to go whenever I can. He said it contributes to the urgency feeling. My pain symptoms are a lot of burning and pressure, leaking, stinging, incontinence, and also urgency, and pain pain pain!! So much pain. Have you not gotten a firm diagnosis yet? If not I would push for a cysto or a potassium test to make sure. Love Sarah
signature: Current meds; , effexor 37.5 mg 2 times a day, and lyrica 100 mg 3 times a day, lots of reading and snuggling with the pets!

contents: I have heard of bladder retraining, where when you feel you gotta go, you try to hold it another 15 min, if u still feel like you have to go then go, if not then go the next time you feel it, but I wouldnt do anything until you've gotten info from your uro/doc.

I do bladder retraining and I feel that it has helped me. I orginally started it after reading many posts from people with teeny tiny bladders that hold ony 150cc or less. I wondered if my bladder might eventually shrink like that. When you say: My symptoms are first a "pressure" in the bladder - pressing feeling, which i have never had before quite like this,, but it is similar to normal urgency with a real bite to it. It is sort of like the urge to urinate as though i have a gallon of h20 in there... then it gets so intense that it hurts. THe pain to me goes into the gential area,, which i suppose is the urethra.. I dont know! This part is like what I feel. But the rest of what you said I don't feel, though I do have burning but it happens very randomly. I also get a sharp pain as my bladder deflates (during urination) on the lower right side right above my pubic bone. I have no idea what that is. If I press on it it hurts like crazy. Last edited by massagedoula; 06-28-2005, 09:57 AM. Reason: fixing
signature: 30 year old Mother, Midwife, Birth Doula, Herbalist, and Massage Therapist with sudden onset of IC approx. 2/16/05 (after a PAP smear and routine gynecological exam). I tried a HUGE list of natural and alternative treatments (including Cystoprotek, Desert Harvest Aloe, Aloe gel, Glucosamine/Chondroitin, L-Argenine, Quercitin/Bromelain, Marshmallow Root...etc) for the first 9 months of having IC, without much relief, until I finally researched and started Elmiron. My experience: ~Previously took Elmiron 100mg three times a day (9/29/05-7/06) ~Experimented with dosage (1-2 Elmiron a day recapped into a plain gelcap) and did well with occasional mild flares. (7/06-9/09) ~Stopped all together at the 1 year mark (9/29/06) for 10 days. Still felt good, with low-level symptoms. ~Decided to go back on it (10/16/06) to see if another year on it will bring me into full (zero symptoms) remission. Currently taking 1-2 pills a day, recapped into a plain gelcap. ~ 6/08...Tried to go off Elmiron again, had huge flare at the 3 week mark, went back on 100mg once a day for maintenance. -I went off Elmiron for 2 months due to pregnancy and had a complete relapse of IC symptoms to pre-diagnosis levels of pain/frequency. I then went back on Elmiron at 10-12 weeks, but was unable to take it further due to severe morning sickness. The 2nd/3rd trimesters were MUCH better IC-wise, and postpartum is the best I have felt in years. I am no longer on Elmiron for now. -20 month postpartum remission from 4/17/09 to 12/10/10. -Out of remission after a pap smear on 12/10/10. Pap smears are obviously the main IC trigger for me. Currently trying to cope with loss of the remission. Also doing: ~Sugar-free/gluten free and strict IC diet (With an emphasis on organic, alkaline and whole foods) ~Prenatal DHA/Fish oil supplement ~Vitamin D supplement ~Culturelle probiotic

contents: I have the opposite problem then you all . I have trouble urinating I have neurogenic bladder and urinary retention my uro says force myself to urinate even if I feel I don't have to because I don't feel when I need to go . Besides the terrible pain and burning in my bladder I feel. I hope you find the answers that you seek. Take care Kim

I haven't had too many troubles lately, my med combo and diet seem to be doing the trick, but I can vividly remember it at it's absolute worst! Mine started out just as "frequent" urination, then progressed to where it felt like my urethra was spasming, very strange feeling. Then along with the spasms came the feeling like I was peeing broken glass, ouch! My bladder was not in the act --- yet. My gyno put me on Ditropan for the spasms/pain, no IC diagnosis yet. That worked for a bit, as by that time it started to feel, as others have described, like I was giving birth to my bladder. I had a need to "bear down" that I could not control, it was awful. But only when I peed. Then it progressed to spasms out of the blue, not just went I voided, and horrible vulvar pain, that felt like things were "swollen" (doc called it "engorgement"). Sometimes the genital ache was so uncomfortable I couldn't sit normal, had to sit like a bird on a perch -- on my feet, it was crazy. Then finally, thanks (ugh) to a UTI my bladder decided to join the party. Then the hell really began! I felt like I had to urinate every 15 min and I was up to 30+ times a day. Plus 4-5 times a night. And what felt like constant spasms. Sometimes I would just be standing there, minding my own business, and it would feel like someone grabbed and pulled/pinched my urethra, it was awful! Mostly, that would happen if I'd been on my feet too long, and at that point just standing in the kitchen cooking dinner was too long! By that point I was put on Elmiron, then a couple weeks later I added in the Vistaril (hydroxyzine) and things have been getting better ever since. Slowly, yes, but getting better. And I would say all of my "symptoms" came on gradually, over the course of about a year, all culminating into "hell" by Nov of '04. Hope my experience helps a little, I know in the beginning, my heating pad and my pyridium were my best friends!! The funny thing is, it took me ages to figure out heat worked for me, it was quite by accident that I found that out (one nice hot shower when I was feeling awful proved it to me). Heat had never worked for me for menstrual cramps, so I never outright tried it on my bladder, thinking it wouldn't help then, wow was I wrong! LOL Take care, and feel better soon! Hugs, Tracey

contents: Hi Ellienor! The fruits in the smoothies that might be suspect would most likely be the banana which is on the "may be ok" list of the IC diet, the canteloupe which is on the "usually problematic" list, and the papaya which also sits on the "usually problematic" list. Try some other IC safe fruit to see if they might be contributing to the discomfort. Like maybe just stick with straight blueberry for a bit of time to see if your symptoms give you a break. Just adding in my 2 cents, for what it's worth! LOL Hugs, Tracey

contents: I describe my pain to a non-icer like this....ever have to pee REALLY bad while on a trip and theres not a potty in sight? The feeling of severe pressure and soreness...making me very aware of my bladder...add to that vaginal pain and swelling and there you have it. I find typically the only relief I have is when I void.When my bladder is flat it is a miserable burning wannna rip my bladder out feeling. I have relief right now for the first time in 6 years...had first hydro in Feb, started elmiron and just had my first instill last week. Believe me, I am trying very hard to get my brain and bladder to remember THIS feeling...since pain 24/7 is all it has had in 6 years! Of course none of us are doctors, so we cannot say for sure if or if not you have ic. But I will tell you what my uro said to me. If you truly have overactive bladder, you typically will not have pain and typically your bladder capacity is normal. If it is just overactive, you will respond to ditropan, Detrol etc. and voila' problem solved! If you have ic my uro NEVER prescribes the above ...first reason being that an ic bladder is NOT overactive it is overly irritated two distict opposites. He feels that those meds mask ic and are not the right therapies...such as elavil,atarax elmiron and hydros. In my case, early on when my general pract. and I thought I had overactive bladder(but really had ic) gave me ditropan. I reacted terribly to it... since my bladder was raw and sore anyway..keeping urine in there longer just made my pain much more intense. It also caused me to have retention and I kept getting utis. In otherwords, my so called overactivity was in reality just a tiny hypersensitive bladder that wanted that horrible stuff(urine) out of there asap...hence chronic frequency. So....my urologist believes in treating ic with the known ic meds first and foremost. Sure sounds like you may have ic dear, just don't give up til you get your diagnoses and the right meds...and you will start feeling better. If I can ever help in any way feel free to pm me anytime Love, Debbie

I use a natural compounded hormone cream made for me, with estrogen,testosterone and progesterone. 'TO PEE OR NOT TO PEE' is definately NOT the question for me!!!!!!! : Big hugs to all my ic friends! contents: My symptoms: menstrual-like cramps or pressure that would not go away; pain or itching in my vaginal area. At first I thought I had vulvodynia, but the cramps/pressure continued, until I went on the IC diet. My uro says Elimiron works in 50% of patients who try it; he did not encourage me to go on it just yet. I find I have a lot of stomach acid. If I don't eat and allow the acid to build up, then my bladder hurts until I urinate the first two times in the morning. So I'm careful not to let too much acid build up in my stomach. As others said, watch out for fruits, because some of them cause problems. Check the 2012 IC Food List for IC-friendly fruits. Also, WATCH OUT for herbal teas. If it is an herbal blend, those usually have black or green tea in them, which will give you major problems if you have IC or a sensitive bladder. Although black and green tea are bad, even some herbal teas can cause problems. I drank some Harney & Sons Camomile tea and got a huge flare from it. I found that Bigelow Camomile Tea does not cause a problem for me. I also drank my tea very strong, which makes things worse. You'd be surprised at seemingly benign foods that will cause pain or flares in somebody with IC. Also, be careful of vitamins--check the ingredients because some may have preservatives or fillers that may irritate your bladder. I found that a lot of probiotics give me flares, as well as multiple vitamins. Good luck and I hope you get checked out---

Hi There, The following recipe - has been helping me lately with flares, especially around times that I have bowel movements that affect my IC. -About a coffee mug amount of Trader Joes Sweetened Vanilla Almond Milk (Almonds -Key ingredient) **You can get any kind of almond milk you desire, if you get regular - add a couple teaspoons of honey -A tablespoon to a tablespoon and a half of Coconut Oil. Also from Trader Joes -A few dashes of Tumeric (Can find this in the seasoning section. Place all ingredients in a pot and warm over stove. And drink! Please let me know on this thread if anyone benefits from it as well. Take care!

Just a note if you are not used to eatting coconut oil, start with a small amount and work up, it is good for keeping your bowels working, but if you start ingesting too much too quick it cam cause MAJOR stomach ache and cramps. I love it though and add it to a hot drink daily. MG

Hi all, I would really appreciate some feedback as I'm getting really worn down & despite trying to be positive I feel this constant flare is finally getting the best of me, I was putting off writing this post because somehow I'm afraid that admitting how bad it is will just make it worse (if that makes any sense) ... I've had IC for well over 10 years now and this is the first time that I've been in a 'flare' for this long (going on 3 months). I record how often I go, pain, etc in a journal every day and there have been only a few "good" IC days throughout this time, the rest of the time I've had a non-stop very strong 'got-to-go' feeling all the time (ex: I go to the washroom & maybe 5 minutes later I feel my bladder bursting) I've tried to stay calm & tell myself, 'ok this will pass' but the longer it drags on the more I worry that maybe it's here to stay (a thought which really scares & depresses me) or, I worry that maybe it's progressing. I'm wondering: Does anyone else have flares that last this long? Also, when people talk about 'flares' I was never sure if they meant that they usually feel AOK bladder-wise but only feel bad when they flare?? (or perhaps you don't feel ok most of the time but a flare is just an exacerbation of this?) -- I'm really not sure... I guess I just really need to hear someone say they've flared this long too and it also went away... Wow, for someone who's had IC for so long I really am amazed at how little I know! Thanks so much, Shelley ps - I take 10 mgs of Atarax a night, & have been going for weekly heparin/sod.bicar/lidocaine treatments & I started the wash-out diet about 10 days ago. I don't know what else to do...

Yup, I've had a flare for 12 months! I just had 1.5 weeks pain free, but its back again! I have been thru Elmiron instills, DMSO/heparin instills, I take Elmiron, Atarax, L-arginine, Detrol LA, had a hydro- trying ANYTHING to break the pain cycle. What my problem is- is that everytime I admit out loud that I feel GOOD, the next day I'm in pain again. Its a sure jinx. I'm discouraged. I usually describe my IC as having a just about constant background pain of 2 to 3 on a 10 scale. Then when I have a flare it goes up anywhere from a 5 to a 9. To put it into perspective, on a 0 to 10 pain scale, childbirth is a 100. So I know that pain can be worse, but why does it have to be here, everyday, bugging me? Sherry PS- You're in Montreal? I'm in West Chazy, NY- just a hour away. I work in Rouses Point.

contents: I've had flares last a long time -- weeks to months. In between flare periods, I guess I feel better, but the IC is always there -- for example, I urinate more than other people even when not in a flare, and I'm prone to back or bladder pain lasting a few hours if I do too much lifting, etc.

contents: Thanks! Hi All, Thanks so much for all your responses, I really appreciate it. It's helpful to know that others have experienced similar flares & that they do go away. After a few months of nothing working it's hard to remember what it felt like to feel OK...guess I'll just have to be patient. Yes, I was tested for a UTI a few weeks ago & nada. Also, I started the treatments nearly 3 months ago (with a few weeks off & then re-started) after the flare started. Before this last flare my IC had actually been REALLY good for months & months... It's also really hepful to know what other's experience of pain is on a scale...I often wonder when I'm reading the posts & others are talking about pain if what I experience is even roughly in the ballpark (b/c pain is such a subjective thing). By the way, thanks for the pain & childbirth comparison..I haven't had children yet so & after reading that I think I may seriously reconsider!!! Hehe! Thanks again & I hope you're all able to find some good moments/days/weeks/ (dare I say...) YEARS! with this illness!! Gosh, I just don't know what to do. Pyridium or AZO doesn't help that much. And, Urimax takes a long time to get through the bladder. Will there be any help for us poor souls to make our life easier? Out of all of you that seem to have continuous flares, do you work? I work 2 days and I can barely handle that. bunnykinb

contents: Hi Everyone, I have recently been diagnosed (about 3 months ago) with IC and I am trying to learn/take all this in. I have been in pain for 6 days now with this recent attack. I had the same question as Shelley did (how long is this going to last). I on the other hand had the UTI. My Dr. put me on antibotics. I take Elmiron and Urelle. Has anyone done the DMSO treatment, if so what is your opinion and did it work for you? Also, my Dr. is suggesting to do catherization w/special soultion (I think like Heprin). Does anyone do that either? If so, does that work for you as well? Thanks for your help!! Rhonda

Bunnykinb, I work full time!!! And it's hard. My bosses hate me. I was like the best employee- worked late, cranked out work. But since having my baby the IC kicked back in full force and I now take a ton of time for uro visits. Sometimes I just don't feel well enough to do certain tasks. I have family medical leave papers filled out saying I have a serious chronic illness. Its a blanket dr note saying that if I'm having too much pain, I can call in. When I started the job 4 yrs ago I found out I had IC 4 weeks prior (had it for a few years, but now the pain had a name.) I had to go to PT for a while for IC and my boss had me working four 10 hr days so that I got my 40 hrs in. Never again, it was too much stress. Rhonda, I have had Elmiron instillations 3x a week for two weeks- didn't work, but they didn't hurt. I just recently went through 6 weeks of DMSO and heparin instills- once a week, and now once a month for at least 3 months. They were brutal!!! They burn and sting (after the ladocaine wears off). I didn't have any improvement during the 6 weeks. A week after stopping the treatments, I felt better. I had 1.5 weeks where I felt great and thought for once my uro was right in torturing me. But I'm right back in my continous flare again. Intercourse may have started it up again, or something I accidently ate- as I'm strict on the IC diet. Any treatment is worth a try. DMSO does make things worse for a while before getting better. Some do have success, I don't think I did. Sherry

contents: Bunny, Have you ever tried UroJet? It's a litocain jelly that you can squirt into your urethra. It's got a generic and it's fairly cheap. That might help. I hope that the Elmirion helps you. It never did me. I've been on a bout for 6 weeks of pretty severe pelvic pain. Find a good doc that is comfortable in pain management. I didn't and now I'm paying the price. I have to wait until July 20 to see a pelvic pain specialist at my local pain clinic. They can prescribe oral meds, physical therapy, local anesthetics... all sortsa stuff. Try a nice hot bath to soak in as much as possible. That and a muscle relaxer or pain med. should fix you up pretty nicely for a while (depending on the medication you take). At least that's what I find most helpful. I'm not taking any meds right now though so I'm in the tub a good portion of the day because it helps the pain while i'm soaking but within 20-30 min after I'm out the pain comes back to exactly what it was - hence the medication suggestion, it should help you for several hours...... just a suggestion.... Caths make my IC flare like nobody's business! My bladder spasms and then every muscle below my ribs go crazy..... so instillations are a last resort for me! I hope yours work!

contents: Bunny, Aren't male cath's bigger? My uro told me they usually use an 18g. on men... I make them use a 12-14 (they are smaller). You could try asking for a pediatric cath. Dont feel bad about not responding to the Hep. instills. I didn't either. I haven't responded to ANYTHING. As for the urojet, it doesn't last a very long time for me. I have heard it last up to a few hours for some though. I mostly use it after sex because I get vaginal burning and that helps A TON!!! (hint: it works even better if it's referigerated first). Just the cold feels soothing. When the urethral burning got bad I've also found a degree of relief from using a "Mr. Freeze" as I've seen it called. Basically what that is: a condom filled with KY jelly and put in the freezer. Then you can insert it vaginally or hold it externally. If it's in the freezer too long it might not be comfortable because it's too cold. I found that keeping them in the fridge in a ziplock bag until I may need it and throwing it in the freezer for only 30 min or so takes it to a good temp for me. It took me a year before I actually got desperate enough to try it but it helped more than anything else ever has for the burning. You don't need to use a lot of KY. I only fill them to the first inch of the condom and tie it off... leaving plenty of room to hold on to the other end (having a "lost condom" situation is bad). I hope those suggestions might help! PS I used to take atarax at night... it didn't help me. Dont be afraid to try another type of medication.

This is a terrible disease, i had never even heard of it before until i got it!! I have heard some talk about taking desert harvest aloe. Has anyone had success with this and what symptoms does it help?

I am having such horrible pain lately that I think I am losing my mind! The instillaiton cocktails only help for a few hours...then the pain returns! I have been taking pain killers which I do NOT normally do. I get side effects forom them but the pain has been wearing me down and I've given into them. I take half of an ultracet just to dull the pain. I am going for an instillation this friday and I think that I will ask the nurse to show me how to self catheter....that's how bad this flare is. It has been going on tooooooooo long! I mentioned Elmiron instillations to my uro but she said that they don't work. I'm not sure what to do at this point. I definately need to talk with her about medications and try to find something that I can tolerate. Something has to give! contents: I know this thread is old but I was reading the posts and see one thing in common... pain. Why is IC so painful? My uro told me there shouldn't be that much pain associated with IC. So why do we all feel it so much? Anyway, I recently went to a doc that specializes in Chronic Pelvic Pain conditions. It turns out that I have several OTHER issues that are attributing to my pelvic pain.... 8 to be exact. Try looking into a local Pain Clinic and see if they have any docs who deal with Chronic Pelvic Pain (IC is considered a chronic pelvic pain condition in most cases). You could probably even get a referral from your OB/GYN. They would probably have a better idea than your urologist, but maybe not. There could be things you didn't know about. I thought mine was all IC too. After my 1st apt I walked out with a great treatment plan to attack all my issues at once to try and aleviate the pain. I would be treated for endometriosis (common in IC patients), myofacial pain syndrome (trigger points), piriformis syndrome, IBS (referred to a GI - also common in IC patients), vulvar vestibulitis (same family as vulvadinia - also common in IC patients), and some others I didn't listen to much. My lengthy point is this: maybe there is something else going on besides IC. It doesn't hurt to check! At worst, you'd have a better doc to treat the pain you have from the IC!
signature: Shelby "The only thing I know is that I know nothing." -- Socrates

contents: Nice to know you found some other sources for YOUR pain..MINE is all inside bladder/urethra indeed. The pain from endo, etc. doesn't hold a candle to it in my situation. Had a bunch of endo removed..was supposed to "fix" me..did abosultely nothing except destroy my urethra from having a cateter in as usual. I do think those with more frequency/urgency ... more like frequent bladder likely do have more muscle involvement, etc. But I can "SEE" where my pain comes from when the huge chunks of tissue come out every time I pee tinged with blood as it has for over five years now. It is UROs like the one mentioned above who prevent those of us suffering from severe BLADDER pain being helped/believed. It is also the reason a pain doc is almost forced to put down more than IC to prescribe pain meds now..because the "experts" have determined there is not that much pain in IC. To that all I can say is BS. I also think that in some cases, far too many patients with overactive bladder..like those you see in the TV ads are being lumped in with IC patients and therefore causing this misconception. All just my own opinion of course. The "gotta go" overactive bladder is NOT IC. Yet much of the community and some patients/docs lump it all together and therefore since those folks have NO PAIN, it is determined that IC presents little or no pain. VERY frustrating.

The longest flare I have had was 8 months and I felt there was no hope. It was a really bad flare too. Horrible pain and frequency to the point I never left the house. On top of it I have RSD in my foot that makes it feel like it is broken all the time and there is nothing wrong with it. So all I did was lay in bed and suffer. I was taking the Elmiron during this time and I think it was that that calmed things down finally. Don't get me wrong I still have flares but they usually last a week or two and the only way to calm things down for me is to cut out most of my usual food on the IC diet and stick to one or two things. Really a pain but that is the only way I can get relief. In my case diet has been the number one salvation and I only have been sticking to it for the past 6 months or so. It seemed too hard to do but you have to stick to it by the letter and don't cheat ever during a flare. It just prolongs it for me. Once I am feeling a bit better I add things in to my diet so I am sure I can identify what is not going to work for me. It is so hit and miss but worth the effort to calm the pain down. I can honestly say in the past 20 years of IC I have some degree of pain every day but since the Elmiron starting helping me the pain is more tolerable.

After two Hydros,(one which had the biopsy) and 12 installions of DMSO and Heparin, I finally broke a 2 year flare. I was in remission for 3 wonderful months before the gremlin reared it's ugly little head again...The Ba ! I just started another round of 6 instills last Friday(August5), hope we can get the gremlin back into remission. If the instills do not work then I will do another Hydro. It appears that I am one of the ones that get some relief from them.
