contents: Constant Pain Just a note to those that have CONSTANT 24.7 BLADDER PAIN - please be sure that every medical staff person you encounter and members here too understand that. I am sick of being told that IC only presents pain during "flares" that come and go. I've had horrific 24/7 pain for over FIVE YEARS NOW with not one second of no pain. With Oxycontin and Percocet daily I still have 6-7 pain levels...ALWAYS. Not everyone with IC has pain only during "flares". Overactive bladder alone is not IC. It is more a "confused" nerve system of the bladder function. My PAIN is indeed from the destruction of the bladder lining which is part of the IC description. Yes mine might be more "extreme" since I can SEE the lining "peeling" constantly and blood far too often but I am not alone with the 24/7 pain issue as we've proven on several threads here. There are some, even members of this community that I wonder if they have more "overactive bladder" than IC. In recent weeks I've read several posts where symptoms seem to line up with those on the Ditropan and other med commercials. Frequency/urgency and "almost accidents" but no pain really other than pressure to go. I have no feeling of the "need to go" because the only signal in that area is horrific pain. I honestly believe many with overactive bladder are now being diagnosed with IC. They went from under diagnosing it to over diagnosing it perhaps? Because of that being included as "IC" now, professionals are believing even less in those of us that suffer from horrific 24/7 pain of the bladder. Sadly, many professionals do not believe that MANY IC patients have CONSTANT pain. The media makes the public believe with those commercials that IC is just "overactive bladder" sometimes because they know people who had those symptoms, were told they had IC and were "fixed" with Ditropan and similar meds. This is NOT to undermine anyone's bladder symptoms - they are all not pleasant. It is just a frustration that those of us that suffer in CONSTANT horrific pain with obvious ongoing destruction of the lining of the bladder hear professionals preach to us that the "pain" is only during "flares" and instead of advancing, it seems we are almost taking a step BACK by blurring the diagnosis boundaries. We've done several threads here that "prove" there are MANY that have constant 24/7 pain not just during "flares". FIVE YEARS STRAIGHT is NOT a flare. It is a permanent condition/symptom. I hope that others who suffer as I do work hard to communicate that FACT to their medical providers and researchers.

Answer for Shelley Hi Shelley, I have had flares last for 12 weeks after feeling quite good, and then getting very depressed about it too! (-: Talk to your doctor! When I am in a flare I will have a urinalysis to rule out a UTI, and in addition to the Elmiron, Detrol LA, and Addarax the bladder instillations help me. My doc has also prescribed Alprolazam to help me sleep at night when a flare is occuring. Do you take Prelief?? Someone recommeded it to me and it seems to help me. Surround yourself with positive people - and if you need to vent, cry, scream - just do it. IC SUCKS and we are all here to listen and offer a shoulder! Good luck! Beth (-:
signature: IC diagnosis 1999 Meds: Elmiron hydroxyzine hcl DMSO as needed valium as needed librax 3x day TENS therapy Thanks to all on the boards - YOU have helped more than I can say! A person's true wealth is the good he or she does in the world.

contents: Since my implant has been out I am experiancing pain on a daily basis. I have also started to develop headaches. I have had 1 migraine and several really bad tension headaches. Another thing to is I can't sleep at night. Anyone else have that problem?! Anyhow I get my implant back in in Sept. I can't wait for the relief. Ditropan doasen't seem to work as well and detrol affects my liver enzymes. I have tryed soma and xanax (alprazalom) it worked well at first but I feel like I am beginnig to need a more potenet dose. Any way Chin up every one I haop eyou all have a good week! Eva

I had a flare for 5 months before I was diagnosed with IC. If I get a mild flare, it lasts for a day. However, I have a bad flare today due to allowing my stomach to remain empty for too long (yesterday I was working on something that has a deadline). So I've had a flare for 2 days now.

My first one lasted 6 months... and then gradually once I started the diet and medication, my flares were further apart and shorter in duration. I have had a few that have lasted for weeks, but have always subsided eventually, but it is extremely depressing especially after you have been feeling ok..... Now my flares are ussually every 6 months (the major ones) I have minor flares here and there but they ussually only last 3 days or so... Hang in there! Jenn
signature: 28 yrs old, I have little to no problems now with my bladder, unless I am very stressed out, I forget to take Elmiron for a few days, or I eat or drink something that I know is a trigger...(i.e) like eating a tomato or drinking tea.... Took a long time to get here, but it IS possible to feel good again I just want people who are new to know that IT CAN get better, and for every one person who is suffering, there are hundreds of others who feel GREAT because they have control of thier IC. What Works: Tylenol 3's, Pyridium, Elmiron (Going on 4 years now!) HOT showers!!! Medications/ Vitamins: Elmiron-100mg 4x's daily Tylenol 3's/Advil (flaring) Vitamin D, WILD salmon oil 4x gels in the morning, Women's ONE a DAY multivitamin (the gummy kind doesn't make me flare) and PROBIOTICS! Me in my graduation gown!

contents: Originally posted by Rhonda Rhonda Hi Everyone, I have recently been diagnosed (about 3 months ago) with IC and I am trying to learn/take all this in. I have been in pain for 6 days now with this recent attack. I had the same question as Shelley did (how long is this going to last). I on the other hand had the UTI. My Dr. put me on antibotics. I take Elmiron and Urelle. Has anyone done the DMSO treatment, if so what is your opinion and did it work for you? Also, my Dr. is suggesting to do catherization w/special soultion (I think like Heprin). Does anyone do that either? If so, does that work for you as well? Thanks for your help!! Rhonda Hi Rhonda. I has the DMSO treatment 6 times and I think it helped but about 10 weeks later I was in a flare and they did one and I started having really bad spasms in my bladder (worse than usual). I can tell you the first one or two may be really painful. I would ask your doctor if he will give you anything for the pain. The garlic after smell is something to keep in mind if you are going anywhere afterwards. It can be pretty strong for a day. Hope this helps!
signature: I am happy to say that I am off all pain medication and currently take Neurontin and Uribel for my IC. I went to the Cleveland Clinic Chronic Pain Rehabilitation program and it saved my life!! I still get bad flare ups but it turns out I was on so many narcotics it was making my pain worse.

contents: This might be a dumb question but what do you mean when people ask if you have urgency. Is it just a feeling of having to go, and then you go and there nothing. I have a feeling somethimes where I have to go but I can hold it for at least a couple hours after I have that feeling. Is that urgency. Can someone please explain that to me.
It's a sudden, have to go NOW feeling. Comes out of nowhere.

contents: Doy you have to really go Do you have to really go but can't after you get that feeling. My feeling comes just like if I have to go to the restroom. I ususally don't go because if I am busy I would rather wait. But the feeling usually leaves but I still fill like I have to go until I really do end up going. If that makes since. I guess what I am trying to say. it doesn't come on that fast ,I can feel it coming. Is that urgency?

That's what i'm feeling right now. Like there is this tickle in my bladder like i need to go but I just went 30 minutes ago and I know I don't have to go. That's the feeling of urgency for me. So I just deal with the uncomfortable tickle until atleast 2-3 hrs go by. I take some tums to try to rid me of the bladder sensitivity. It's really annoying. Deana

Its seems like I go more in the morning It seems like i go more in the morning. Is that thrue for anyone else. I guess I am asking this question because I told my doc that I really don't have to bad of urgency. I really didn't consider me feeling like I have to go once in a while urgency because it is not like I feel like I have to go right then. What to you guys think.

I also go a lot in the mornings..I spend almost every 5 minutes going in the morning..it will start to slow down around 1:00 p.m. for me. I have the urgency that is .."I have to go now and that is means NOW"...if I don't the bladder will not hold. So for me it means now..and if I don't then I run the risk of my bladder emptying on its own. I never hold my urine more than I have to time wise..as my uro said that makes matters worse and to make sure you don't do that..he told me that years back. I also am one that drinks alot of water to keep my urine diluted as I have the Hunner Ulcers and it helps and he told me it would help. For me urgency means.."Go NOW"..I say that to my friends that don't have IC..and they know I mean NOW. I live where I can be caught in gridlock in traffic for long periods of time and if we have snow it can get real bad..so another friend that uses pads for a situation told me about using them..so I will do that..if I know I run the risk of getting caught in gridlock where our traffic is at a dead standstill because I won't be able to wait for a off ramp or anything else..that is only when desperate though that I will result to that. Dusty

Urgency? >But the feeling usually leaves but I still fill like I have to go until I really do >end up going. If that makes since. I guess what I am trying to say. it >doesn't come on that fast ,I can feel it coming. Is that urgency? Urgency is when you can not wait. That feeling does not leave, it just gets worse and worse until you do go. Were you ever on a long trip and had to go but needed to wait. It really started to hurt trying to hold it. That's the way I would explain the "gotta go now". Ginny

Ginny, I long trips that is when my friend for sure uses pads and just lets her bladder go. I asked her how she taught herself to do this and she said it was better than the pain and all..then she would stop when she can and change. What you are describing sounds like pressure..and I have had that too..you feel like you have to go but can't. That is a miserable feeling. You also may not be emptying all the way..can you tell if your empty. You may be holding residule. Pressure is very miserable. Are you sure you don't have a infection maybe? Dusty
Don't feel like its a dumb question, its good to ask questions that your not sure about.

no question is ever dumb... unless of course your asking my dogs to watch my dinner and not eat it ok.. being funny... urgeny, is when you have to go and you mean right this second... and if you can't go you want to bust... never feel that you are asking a dumb question...... Brat
signature: 'The will of God will never take you where the Grace of God will not protect you.'

contents: I too have been wondering if what I experience is urgency. I always feel like I have to go. I could go, and feel like I have to go again 10 seconds later. I know that it will only be a few drops or nothing at all, but if I at least go a few drops, I feel better- for a little bit. The feeling always comes back again very soon. This happens to me ALL THE TIME- it is constant. It is like a pressure/pain. It makes me crazy!! I know that I am not having residue and I am empty because I have had test to make sure that was not my problem. I don't have to go I just FEEL like I have to go! It is so terrible!! This is my main IC symptom.
Oh yeah- it is not like I have to go "right now" though. Also, I never lose control of my bladder or have leaking.

I've resorted to using pads on long trips and times when I have a meeting and I know getting to the bathroom will be a problem. I was at a meeting this weekend and someone said to me, I hope you feel better, it's hard to be at a meeting and feel ill. Another asked if I was alright.. I hate that. I have some friends who really understand and don't ask questions.

Urgency....a feeling that comes on suddenly of having to go and normally means...go or a leakage. It it is normally caused by bladder spasms. If you have you get a feeling of having to go all the time (as I do) it may be because of spasms too....as both symptoms may be a sign of bladder spasms. It may be because of inflammation alone...but but an inflammed bladder does also increase your risk of bladder spasms. Have you ever tried some anti spasmodics? By the way...mornings are often my worse time these days...since I usually still tired plus have held it quite a while. I find that taking some kind of ant acid such as prilosec otc that works long hours has helped lesson how acidic my morning urine is. ((((((((Noelle)))))))) Ask questions all you want....the better we understand our disease the better we can treat it. We can explain things to our doctors better and we can do what we can for ourselves better. Plus...whenever we ask questions on this board the answers are here to help others besides just us! ((hugs))

contents: I always say to people when they say,"Can I ask a dumb question?" NO question is dumb question...thats true. I like I said have urgency and it is "I got have to go and I have to go now." If not I will have leakage or a bladder that empties on it's own...so when I say go now ..I mean go now. I had my bladder fixed also, but still will run into this problem...especialy in a flare..which I am in right now..and as usual it follows my estrogen shot I give myself..and what ever else at this time I have not clue..I just know I have had it right now with pain and running to the bathroom. Between the spinal cord injury,the bladder which has the Hunner Ulcers, my knee injury on my good leg...feels like all I have done is live in pain lately..I seldom cry and last night I was.."I am so tired and at the end of my rope on this pain stuff." I am also heading into the anniversary of my husbands death..which is May 19th and already feel the pain from that..then sick on top with so many things causing me severe pain....I am usually upbeat and all...not right now..I just want to sit down and cry as it seems never ending for me. The 19th is my birthday and I don't want to celebrate it and have told people so..my husband died on my birthday and I am just not interested. I am in one of my worst flares and can't get rid of it. I sound awful don't I? That is seldom me. It is like come on body give me a break. I burned so bad last night I wanted to just die..figure of speech so don't let it worry you. I take morphine for pain control for my spinal cord injury, but after a day or so it gives me a severe headache..so have the headache also today, as woke up with it. I don't know why that drug does that for me, but it will after so many days. I miss my hubby as he was so great and so understanding because he knew himself what it was like to be sick and all and go through bad things in life..he did so in Viet Nam..that left him with two permanent injuries and so on..and with his cancer..and we went through these illnesses of ours together. I know he is about and all...I just wish he was here right now as he understood what it was like when all my illnesses caused trouble at once. Sorry did not mean to go into so much detail, but I had noticed I was botteling it as I did not want to cry. Dusty
Urgency for me is: I HAVE TO GO RIGHT NOW or I LEAK and can't hold it. It comes on all of a sudden and I have to go right now, I have pressure and I can't think of anything else until I go pee!

Oh Dusty, I'm so sorry! You've been through so much! For me, I normally feel like I have to go. Like my bladder's always sending constant, but subtle signals that it really needs to be emptied, even though I know there's next to nothing in there. My urgency feeling comes on suddenly, a screaming signal saying to my brain; if you don't get this out of me right now I'll let loose right here and now! -And from experience I know my bladder isn't making an idle threat, -it will do it! Even with the urgency signal, it still has nothing to do with the amount of urine actually in my bladder. It's wierd. Vicki Last edited by VickiB; 05-15-2005, 08:03 AM. Reason: addition
signature: "The happiest people don't necessarily have the best of everything; they just make the best of everything they have."

contents: "Urgency" means always feeling like you have to go. Then when you do go you still feel like you need to go more. And when you do try again barley anything comes out.
signature: cyndip

contents: That's how I feel. My urgency is constant; a painful/pressure feeling of needing to go all the time. It does not come on all of a sudden. I'm not in fear of losing control of my bladder. But it's still awful. Even if I can ignore it and get through the day I feel extremely uncomfortable and miserable. I can urinate but the "need to go" feeling still doesn't stop. I'm not sure if I'm voiding everything I need to (haven't been tested yet) or if my nerve functioning is simply out of whack.

ok so not sure if this is the right place to post this or not, but I definitely think this info would be pretty life changing for some of you ladies. DIY Padsices Rub aloe vera gel on a pad add about a teaspoon of witch hazel (non-alcoholic) sprinkle some lavender essential oil then just wrap up in tin foil and put it in the freezer found this on pinterest for post partum. Def getting this alll together and trying it out tmrw night. but as always, we should be careful of what we put on our girly parts, we all know it takes the randomest thing to flare and be left hating life. Switch it up if you know that you are sensitive to one of the things. Here is the original link: Page not found - http://intheknowmom.net/?p=19294

You also might try getting non-chlorine pads/tampons. I didn't even know they had chlorine in them until I started researching IC. It had made a world of difference in not having flares during my period.
I've started using reusable cloth pads during my period and have noticed less irritation during my period. They sell some on the IC site here or you can do an online search for some.

contents: Briza, I just read this for the second time, and loved it again. I also sent it to my son who is struggling with some major issues at the moment. Thanks for posting the blog. Laurie

Aloha everybody! 8 months ago, when I started having problems with pelvic/vaginal pain, initially I thought I had vulvodynia/vulvar pain (VP). I had suffered a bout of VP three months after giving birth to my son, and was prescribed Hydrocortisone Cream. I had some Hydrocortisone Cream leftover, so when I began to experience what I thought was VP, I used it and got relief. I've since learned I have IC, and not VP. My flares take the form of pelvic pressure/pain and vaginal pain. Yesteday I was going through a bad flare, so I used the Hydrocortisone Cream and my flare went away. I don't know if this will work for everybody. My IC was caused by too much acid in my stomach, not eating enough, and prolonged stress (I've been dealing with liquidating my parents' estate for 2 1/2 years). If you can get your doctor to prescribe the Hydrocortisone Cream for your flares, give it a try. I use it 2x day when I have a flare and it seems to really help relieve the pain.

IMHO, one of the most empowering things an IC patient can do is learn to do instillations herself. I taught myself to do it, and also made the solution myself, because at the time I had no doc to help me. The "cocktail" recipe I have been using is the lidocaine-bupivicaine-heparin-bicarb one posted here under (I think) "Physician Resources". For nine years, this worked perfectly for me for every flare - it was like flipping a switch that turned the pain OFF. It is NOT a cure, by any means, and may not work forever - but for me it worked for nine years, and let me continue working for two more years.
What exactly is an instillation and how does it work?

(This is from Marti... she was having trouble posting it!) Well Hi "neighbor" - We aren't that close geographically, but a lot of my family lived in Boulder Creek. I see that you are new to the ICN forum, so I hope someone will point you to some of the discussions about instillations that have occurred in the past. Donna, ICN Moderator is somewhat of an instillation expert, and has moderated several discussions you would find instructive. Instillation - some urologist still use the older term "gavage", which just means washing - is direct introduction of medication/therapeutic agents into the bladder by means of a catheter. It may sound rather invasive at first, especially to do to yourself, but it saved my life. I mean that literally - I was headed toward becoming part of the suicide statistic because the pain was so unbearable and I had no one to help me. The reason the various lidocaine-based instillation "cocktail" recipes are referred to as "rescue" strategies is that they turn the pain off instantly, just as an injection of lidocaine, a local anesthetic, numbs up an area where you have a wound stitched up, or as procaine is used by the dentist. It is really quite miraculous - or it seemed so at the time. I had been in the most unbearable agony and when I put the lidocaine "cocktail" into my bladder, it was like ripping a switch - the pain was gone! I remember lying on the bathroom floor, sobbing with relief because I realized I had a way to control the pain, and therefore continue my life. This was in 2003. As for the nuts and bolts of the procedure, Donna or one of the patients who have been taught self-instillation in urologists' offices would be a better source of info, because I don't really know how it is done in urologists' offices. You need a catheter, purchased from medical supply houses - you need a prescription for these, believe it or not!!! - and then some way to introduce the solution into the catheter. Here's where you'll need instruction from Donna or some other experienced member who was trained by a urologist's nurse, because my method was just cobbled together out of stuff I had in my lab (I am a research toxicologist). You will need a mirror and a light like a gooseneck desk lamp. Some people sit on the toilet, some people put one foot on the toilet seat (I'm not sure how they arranged the mirror and lamp); I sit on a cushion on the floor; and then you insert the catheter into your bladder about 4 to 6 cm. That's just enough until urine comes out; you want to avoid poking the bladder wall.* After all the urine is drained, you put the solution in. Some people put the bicarb in with the lidocaine etc mixture, but I put the bicarb in a separate syringe because it causes precipitation if you mix them all together. If you don't spend a lot of time in your "downstairs" you may have to familiarize yourself with the specifics of female anatomy down there to locate the urethra - it's kind of an elusive little bugger. Here's a pretty good diagram: Female Genitalia http://catalog.nucleusinc.com/generateexhibit.php?ID=4508 This medical exhibit pictures the primary anatomy of the external female genitalia shown from a perineal or saddle (inferior) view. This single image features a view from the inner thighs to the medial structures of the vagina and anus. Labels identify the urethra, vagina, anus, clitoris, labia majora and labia minora. Of course, no one looks exactly like the illustration (or we'd all order ourselves from catalogs, hey?) but it will help you aim. If you've had a kid or several kids, you'll doubtless have gotten a bit re-arranged. Practice a few times, and be glad you're not a guy, whose urethras are so long it gives me the willies (no pun intended) to think about sticking a catheter in one of them. Speaking of guys (or willies) there are some women whose husbands give them instillation treatments. It depends on the nature of your relationship I guess - the only time I had to ask for help from my husband, I thought I'd die of mortification. But it was slightly better than dying of pain. We both survived the experience. So you have to find a urologist who supports patient self-instillation because the "cocktail" ingredients are all prescription drugs. My situation was very unusual because we have our own lab, so I just did it on my own. Then you have to assemble your equipment, and then you have to choose a "recipe" - probably the urologist will tell you what his/her favorite recipe is. Here are some frequently used: http://www.ichelp.org/page.aspx?pid=937 http://www.ic-network.com/treatments...illations.html 404 Not Found https://www.ichelp.org/document.doc?id=20 And if you google "bladder instillations" you'll retrieve a bunch of patient experience testimonials as well. One note: Many protocols say "Retain the solution for 5-to 20 minutes, then void it out. Screw that - hold it for as long as you can. Volume doesn't matter, but time does. So does pH - pH matters a LOT, so don't try to get creative with the bicarb. cheers marti

contents: Hi all. I dont know if I have IC or not, but I do have frequent urination. My other big problem is hemorrhoids, which cause me to become constipated which makes me have to urinate more. Anyway, I have an appt set up with a urologist in a few days and im just wondering if urologists normally treat hemorrhoids or would I have to see my regular doctor for that? if anyone has any info on this i would appreciate it. thanks

Don't quote me on this, but I doubt it. I think you would see a surgeon, but probably the specialist I would see is a Gastroenterologist, as they would biopsy them, etc...I am not sure, but I just don't think UROs deal in this....if I am wrong let me know. Good luck, it's a miserable cycle you are in and that is nasty pain....you might be advised to try stool softeners in the meantime, but do get advice on these...also keep your water intake up there, it will help with constipation....lots of things impact that, but diet and fluids are as important as some of the meds that cause them. Good luck, hope you find relief soon. Last edited by Betsie; 04-01-2005, 11:49 AM.

contents: Your PCP will probably tell you to go get some Colace (generic) at the drug store. It's over the counter. You can take more than 2 at a time (per my doctor). It worked for me and my PCP says it always takes about a month to clear up just due to how much wear and tear goes on down there.

Im worried about it doing damage, or that I will get into a flare that will take me a while to get back from, has anyone had this experience and switched to anything else that was less harsh but in the same family.
Yeah, I flare from the antibiotic metronidazole.

YES this is my number one flare trigger. I flare HORRIBLY on any antibiotic, and so I stay away from them all together, I hope that I never have to take them ever again! I was given doxy for a suspected micoplasma infection last year, and that is the most pain I have ever been in. Jenn
signature: 28 yrs old, I have little to no problems now with my bladder, unless I am very stressed out, I forget to take Elmiron for a few days, or I eat or drink something that I know is a trigger...(i.e) like eating a tomato or drinking tea.... Took a long time to get here, but it IS possible to feel good again I just want people who are new to know that IT CAN get better, and for every one person who is suffering, there are hundreds of others who feel GREAT because they have control of thier IC. What Works: Tylenol 3's, Pyridium, Elmiron (Going on 4 years now!) HOT showers!!! Medications/ Vitamins: Elmiron-100mg 4x's daily Tylenol 3's/Advil (flaring) Vitamin D, WILD salmon oil 4x gels in the morning, Women's ONE a DAY multivitamin (the gummy kind doesn't make me flare) and PROBIOTICS! Me in my graduation gown!

contents: pmap, i have heard it gets worse before it gets better with dmso instills. what kind did ya get? dixie has good luck with heparin. nothing for me so far, except a little less peeing, but the pain and pressure are bad still. the urgency comes back after abot 5 days. it doesn't last for me. hang in there...... dixie, happy b-day late..... shenna

/comfort Yes, for me in the beginning, they hurt and I needed to urinate all the time. I also sometimes had flares afterward. Unfortunately, mine were done at the same time they were brute force stretching my urethral opening, so that only increased the problems. It took some time, but most of the time now, they don't hurt and the increased urge doesn't last more than a day. You might try taking Uristat before hand so it numbs things a bit. Maybe even a pain killer, if you have them. But it did take months to reach the level of semi comfort I now have with them. I think they have helped, but it wasn't easy to keep going when they were hurting. One thing my uro did for me was to numb me both before and after the treatment, which often made it possible to hit home before the pain and urgency hit. Currently, I am able to hold the DMSO on average of two to three hours before I have to urinate. If urgency hits, sitting on my trusty icebag stops the need. So, for me, it is working and I have a lot more good days than bad. :woohoo: Hang in there, it could be worth the effort and pain, but do talk to your doctor about it. Myli

I have been doing installations for about two weeks now. I have been peeing like crazy since my second one. My first one I did really well with. I can actually eat anything without getting pelvic pain. I hope it gets better. I hate having to pee all the time. Chelsey
I have been getting instills for about 6 months now for me it was a bit uncomfortable in the beginning but I do get some relief from from them now. Good luck and hope your feeling better, Kelly

contents: Rimso is the name for DMSO. It is usually instilled once a week for 6-8 weeks initially, then if it helps, once a month for awhile after that. The first few treatments are pretty well known to make you flare, but after the first few treatments are over, it can get better. I've never heard of having DMSO more than once a week... (Heparin? yes, every few days, I've heard, but never heard that for DMSO). I have been helped by the DMSO cocktail (Rimso, Heparin, Solucortef, Sodium Bicarbonate & Marcaine). I hold it for 20 minutes, then void. When I was having the initial series of 6 treatments done, I took Darvocet about 2 hours beforehand & Pyridium the morning of the instill. Those helped me be able to hold it the 20 min. (The first one I could only hold 10 min). I noticed some small improvement around the 4th treatment, but the bigger improvement came about 10 days after the series was over. I have less pain & less frequency now. My energy level is better & I sleep better. I am now able to do some exercise, which I could not do before this. I am still limited in diet, which is disappointing, but have no regrets. I don't think I really realized how sick I was til I started feeling better. I wish you much success with your treatment & better days soon!

contents: installments So I was just diagnosed with IC I am so glad I found this site I was starting to think all this pain was in my head, and the Dr. just made this up to satisfy me since they had done every test there was and found nothing. I was diagnosed with Fibromyalsia about a year ago and have alot of symtoms due to that. When I was diagnosed with IC I was upset all I could think was what next? I had my first installment last week and go for another one on Thursday. I have to have 4 installments 1 time a month I am wondering how well these treatments are working for you guys and if you think there is anything else other than the treatments. I still have severe pain and I am really hoping these treaments work. Does anyone else on here have fibromyalsia also? My Dr. told me these go hand in hand.

I had a installation and it was awful for me. The pain was bad. But maybe it will work for you. I told my DR that I do not want to do it again. I was in so much pain that my husband had to take two days off from work to take care of me. I do hope it works for you

installation I guess it went better for me just because a week before the installation I had to have a scope of my bladder done..now that is pain my friend.. But seriously the installations are painful but I am in serious abdominal pain and anything has to be better then constantly being in pain. I feel very uninformed right now though and I thought this was a condition that would go away after the installations only to read on the internet today that it is a condition that will haunt me for the rest of my life. I am trying hard to stick to the diet but finding it very difficult since all the stuff I love I can no longer have! Did your doctor put you on oral medications to help you instead of the installations then?

Hi, yes she did. I see a PA instead of my uro. I only see him when I have surgery. I feel lucky to have her since she is a women and understands the female body. When I take Darvocet and Lortab when it gets really bad. And at night I take Vestral. Do you feel like you have IC brain? You can not remember things and get confused? I have been that way ever since I came down with IC.

Pa I would like to see a doc a little closer to me right now I drive a hour to and from the appt. I have to go once a week. It is tough and I hope that the pain gets better I never thought about asking for pain medicine but I am goign to when I go in Thursday because I just cant take the pain anymore. as far as the IC brain goes LOL I am fuzzy all the time I forget everything, I read that it may be from the fibromyalsia! I am just glad they diagnosed me with something I think my regualar doc was starting to think I was coo coo. I have also read that just 4 installments may not work either..so now I am kinda freakin out thinking this is never going to go away. I get constant headaches also is this something that you have also?

Yes I have headaches all the time. My family Dr thinks its because of my age LOL. I finde that the pain meds do help with the IC. Also stress can cause flars. I try to stay stress free but I'm type of person thinks she can help people like my family. So why do you have to go once a week to your URO? My drive is about 30 mins.

My doc thinks that if I can get the inflamation under control then the headaches may go away. I have installations every week for 4 weeks, thats why I have to go in every week. Stress is a part of my daily life my husband is newly diagnosed with MS and my oldest son is disabled I tell people I wouldnt know how to function without stress LOL..

signature: 27 year old student Had symptoms since I was 5 Been on forums since '07, knew I had this since then, received mild treatment without true diagnosis due to anxiety. Diagnosed by symptoms on 4/08/11. Had first horrible flare in 07, bad doctors, had to research IC myself to clue in onto what it was. After following the diet I went into remission. Big flare in 09 due to an untreated bladder infection. Went back into remission in 2012. Symptoms returned 4/1/15. All of my remissions have ended in April-May or September-October. It sounds like a connection to seasonal allergies to me.. Currently: Continuous Birth Control, vaginal valium (10/6/11), Elmiron (4/08/11), UTA (4/08/11), continuous Trimethoprim for infections (10/6/11), Heating pad, Benedryl, harsh diet What I know works: Benedryl, diet, birth control, UTA, valium, ditropan, soma What didn't work: Elavil, vistaril (12/22)

contents: thanks nekura i am on pecocet what is the pill called that makes your urine blue? i personally wouldnt recommend the installations to anyone my experience was pretty bad and very painful im to the point where i just want my dr to take my bladder out...... everything they have tried just causes more pain now im down to last resorts

I was 12 when I first started showing symptoms of IC. I lived 9 years with a very mild case and then when I was 20 I went from mild to moderate overnight. I am constantly worrying about my IC progressing more and becoming severe. Right now I am always in pain, but I can deal with it by staying busy. If my case became worse, if I lost my ability to work and do the things I love -- it would be the worst thing that could happen to me. Whenever I experience a flare, I put myself into a state of panic, convinced that this is the day my IC becomes forever worse. All the worry and dread does not help the flare, of course. The day I awoke to a full-blown case of IC was the worst day of my life. It has taken me over a year to come to terms with it. I don't know if I would be strong enough to handle getting worse again. I know that IC is not classified as a progressive disease and that many people never progress. But I think I worry about it so much because I have already progressed from mild to moderate. When I imagine my future in 10 years, it terrifies me to see myself as homebound, dependent on narcotics, and living on the toilet. I try to live in the moment as much as I can. I am grateful for the strength I've gained and for the fact that I'm able to live a great life in spite of IC. But there is always the nagging worry that IC could shatter it all once again. I guess I'm wondering if anyone else has this fear, and how you deal with it.

contents: For me, like you, my IC HAS been progressive, at least over the last five years. (I read somewhere that some doctors think that IC may be progressive for the first several years, then most cases kind of reach the state they will remain in...so hopefully that might be the case in your case, those doctors don't believe most people get worse after the first five years or so...) What has helped me is knowing that I am willing to do whatever it takes - up to and including bladder removal - to not give up to this disease. I am going to try every possible remedy out there until I find what works for me, and if it takes bladder removal, then that's what I will do. What helps me is knowing that I refuse to give up and live with this disease. I will always be fighting to get better. Blessings, Lori

Living in the "Land of IF" Originally posted by megancolleen I am constantly worrying..... If ...my case became worse, if ... I lost my ability to work and do the things I love -- it would be the worst thing that could happen to me. Whenever I experience a flare, I put myself into a state of panic ... ... convinced that this is the day my IC becomes forever worse. I don't know if I would be strong enough to handle getting worse again. But I think I worry about it so much because..... When I imagine my future in 10 years..... I try to live in the moment as much as I can..... But ... Dear Megan Colleen, I just read your post and I hope that the highlighting above will help you to reorganize your thoughts and do a little 'proving' before you allow yourself the luxury of spending even just ONE MORE MINUTE in the Land of IF. There is a great exercise you can do to help counter this kind of thinking. Cognitive Thought Stopping is a great technique that you can try. Make yourself a big STOP SIGN and when you hear yourself start to wonder IF something is going to happen -- STOP -- and tell yourself that WHEN something happens you will deal with it -- but you won't allow yourself the luxury of worrying until it actually happens. Place stop signs in every room -- and when you hear your inner voice asking IF, look at the sign (sight) and say the word (sound) and you will help yourself to get a grip on this inner negative thinking. When you see the stop sign, redirect your focus to something pleasant, and say peace or relax to yourself... it will soon be happening more than the IF thinking will. Another really helpful suggestion is to PROVE your statements: When you worry about an IF situation: PROVE IT... for instance you wrote.. "If my case got worse, and if I lost my ability to work, it would be the worst thing that could happen to me..." To PROVE that statement you can take the result -- "...the worst thing that could happen to me" and PROVE THAT TO BE TRUE!!! and start to try to prove it by putting on paper other things that you can think of that WOULD be worse --- for instance, I think that having an arm or a leg amputated would be worse than not being able to work or having a bad case of IC. I also think that losing my spouse or my child would be worse.. If fact, hearing that I had CANCER would be worse too for me... You try it... fill in the blanks when you tell yourself saying these worrisome thoughts... When you downsize that worry, you will be amazed that you will stop living in the Land of IF and start LIVING the life you are in without wasting so much time in the LAND that doesn't really exist anyplace but in your own mind. I sure hope this will help you to stop the worrying... PM if you would like more suggestions like this!!! HUGS, prayers and love, Mary

My first symptoms were when I was 13. Very Mild case. Than I entered the age of infections and got worse.....after I finally got diagnosed and correct treatment I got better and better. Certain things have set me back along the way....abdominal surgeries, stress, and treatments not working out. Regardless....I improved after each time I had a set back. I am very close to remission as far as the IC goes right now. It seemed to me like I progressed along the way but I wasn't yet diagnosed, so I wasn't doing things right for me, and each time I got significan'ty worse there was something I can connect it to. I think getting worse prior diagnosis has a lot to do with not knowing what to do to prevent problems. I do not think that loosing your ability to work is the worse thing that can happen. If it does I think you will be able to deal with it. It changes things but you will find a path that fits your circumstance. It seems terrible because this world focusses so much on work and money...neither of which are really a path to real happiness.....you can have a very happy and fullfilling life IF you have to face that....but at this point I think you should live for today and not worry about tomorrow. IF you do have to face not being able to work at some point...for any reason not necessarily IC...I would think you would want to live the time you had to its fullest. Because I had other illnesses that were progressive I suspected my IC would be the same....especially since it seemed I had a mild case for sooo long without a diagnosis. It was a fear that had me very upset. It turned out to be wrong. My IC was not progressive and did actually improve with time. Unfortunatly it took some real time to really see that. I do believe that whatever the future holds you can handle it....your not alone. I also believe that you will find a treatment plan that helps you to improve with time. it will be ok!

contents: My IC started when I was just a little girl, in 1966...over the decades it did progress...in the 60's the dr's didn't know what I had....small bladder...kid is just trying to get more attention..you know,,,typical dr crap when they don't know what's wrong with you, so it must be your fault, and it doesn't matter if you are just a little bitty kid that is in pain.......I was diagnosed in 1976 but the only treatment then was DMSO...that didn't work..burned, oh yes...helped, oh no.....made it through school during the 70's by peeing every change in classes and right before getting on the bus, and before and after lunch.....through the 80's I married and had a child...IC got a little worse every year through the 80's and 90's.....in 2004 it was so bad I couldn't live with it anymore...I saw a wonderful dr that tried everything ( Except Interstim,,,did NOT want that, no, no, no,no)..finally we had to admit defeat and I had my bladder removed in Dec...after a very long recovery with lots of complications, I have finally got the green light to " Lesa, have a nice life".....So...I am planing on taking college classes to become an RN, while working as a CNA to help pay those darn bills...
signature: After suffering with IC for 40 years, my bladder was removed in Dec 05...YAY!

contents: mine progresses too... for about 2-3 years it was really mild, i ate and drank whatever i wanted, etc..barely noticed it at all. then one day it flared up and it hasnt been the same ever since. i now have to watch my diet carefully to avoid more pain. it is very upsetting and discouraging. I also freak out when I get a bad flare that lasts 2 weeks or longer (ive been getting those lately). I am just trying to live life one day at a time and not think too far ahead. I am working and I am in law school. it is tough, but I will never forgive myself if i drop out of school in my current condition. I am still somehow able to get through the semesters. and make it to work. I know there is a chance I will not even be able to practice law when I am done (I still have 3 years, part-timer) but it is a chance I am willing to take because this has been my dream forever. In the meantime, I am being as proactive as possible about my healthcare.. I am trying new things and keeping the diet, allowing myself to stop and rest when I need to, and trying to keep the stress down. I pretty much gave up my social life, which is hard as I was always sociable and enjoyed going out, but it had to go. I am also seeing a therapist and starting yoga soon. I think that IC can get better (we see it on here all the time) and it helps to just hope and pray that you will too.. your not alone, I am right there with you

Just the simple truth regarding fear. I simply PM's Megan about my case and explained that my worst fears did happen to me but that I prevailed and I'm alive and for the most part as happy as I can be for today. Kara

contents: You said that you are trying to live in the here and now. That is great attitude, but it is normal to project about the what if's. I was one of those who it did progress in. I had it since I was a toddler, so for me I never knew any different about not running to the bathroom. I was diagnosed in 1978 at the age of 22. I tried every treatment available wiht no success. So in 1986 at age 30 I had my bladder out. It was the best thing that could have happened to me. I finally was able to sleep through the night for the first time in my life, could actually go on long car trips and we did not have to stop every half hour for a bathroom break. I was able to live the life I wanted to live and be the type of mom I wanted to be to my children. You are very lucky in that are coming out with new treatments all the time- they are actually doing research on IC. When I was diagnosed many doctors did not believe it existed. I did not have many options to try- this was before Elmiron, Interstim, the IC diet, etc. Continue to live in the moment and trust that what ever happens you will get through it.It has been 21 years since I had the surgery and I have not regretted it. Judith

I think mindfulness meditation and yoga are excellent tools to learn to live in the here and now and accept whatever happens with open hearted compassion for it as well as yourself. Live for now. If things are going good go with that. If things are not going good, believe that they will get better.
signature: What helps Elmiron 200 mg bid Wellbutrin XL anxiety and depression Oxybutynin ER 10 mg Yoga tried and failed Cardura, Hytrin, Flomax, Prosed DS, Vesicare, Pyridium, Cystoprotek (caused GI problems), Lyrica, Pamelor "We can't wait until the storm is over. We need to learn to dance in the rain."

contents: I am with Donna completely on that one. I know I am going to have flares and bad days and also good days and when I have a good day I run with it. I make the best of my good days and my bad days I rest and spend my day usually in the bathroom non stop, but I have learned ways to control my symptoms better, bladder instills and really sticking with the diet because if I cheat, I will have a bad day! I was down the shore this past summer and I love carmel appled with nuts, I LOVE them, I know I can't have them and I had one so that night and the next day I had bladder pain so bad I could not walk, so I know my limits and I know how to try and better control it as best as I can. I still have my bad days because every day I wake up and I know my bladder is there because I can always feel some pressure no matter what I do, but I have this and all I can do is try and protect my bladder by eating the non harmful foods, taking my meds and doing the best I can. I have a very hard time with food, I do not tolerate much and I miss alot of things I cannot have but if it makes me feel better, I will stick with my pears that I can now have finally since taking lyrica! yay And stick with what works for me. I believe the more we protect our bladders the better we will be, its hard sometimes, sometimes I want that candy bar or I want that BLT with tomatoes and mayo, but I learned to find other foods that are good that I can have and now I can eat out and enjoy my dinner, without hurting my bladder. I really don't fear mine getting worse at this point, I had a uro who once told me, if you take care of your bladder and follow the diet and take elmiron your bladder should not get worse, he said the good thing about IC is it usually, I say usually does not progress, but the pain can worsen because of the foods you eat and the damage you can do, therefore you can be in control of it, if you choose to eat what your not supposed to, then you take the chance of it irritating your bladder, that kind of thing. Mine can be pretty bad sometime and it has been this way since 1999 so I dont see it worsening at this point, I see it getting better as I follow the diet and take my meds.
signature: Lisa Current meds:Elavil, Lortab as needed, Vivelle dot hormone patch 0.1mg, estrace cream, lidocaine ointment, pepcid Diagnosis: IC in 1999 , IBS, Pelvic floor dysfunction, vulvodynia, vestibulitis, chronic low back pain, scoliosis, hunners ulcers on bladder, hysterectomy 2/08, fibro 3/08, sciataca

contents: Stay positive I too fear mine will get worse one day, at the moment my frequency is just above normal say about six to seven times a day. I can live with that, I do have flares when the pain is pretty bad and I feel so deppressed and annoyed. Im only twenty three and its a lot to deal with, I am half way through college, and yes there are somedays I wonder will I be able to work if this condition gets any worse. The way I handle my fears is just to say to myself, now life is not that bad, every one has fears about the future even those without ic. The thing is to try and enjoy the present, and make the best of each day, I am lucky my boyfriend of four years has stood by me, through my battle with deppression, and ic. Its not easy,and I do wish I could enjoy sex without pain, or the fear of a flare, and maybe that it will all get too much for him someday. For now I am trying to be positive, Im not on any meds, but I am following the diet and drinking marshmallow root tea, as well as practising accupressure. I do also pray, and I think my faith really helps.

I guess I was lucky when I was diagnosed because there wasn't a lot of information out there. I don't remember my doctor ever saying there wasn't a cure yet. I was 23, had 2 young sons and a very supportive husband. My husband is the one that actually did the majority of the looking up info on the internet because I had not slept in days because I was up and down trying to empty my bladder. My uro had mentioned cystitus and he found the ICA association and we sat there and read my symptoms that I had written down, which of course was the constant need to void, pressure and those lovely needs to pee again before I even had my pants zipped up. I didn't know what to expect there in the beginning. I was a very busy person with working full-time, just receiving a great promotion and there really wasn't a way to not take care of my kids. My IC was mild to moderate at first and my doctor was very good at changing up my meds to get me to the point that I could function fairly normally. It took alot to keep me down. And my husband knew when I was really having a bad day, that I was going to slow down or even just stop. I had enough depression while growing up, so I guess I knew I wasn't going to let that disease get me down. I was a great pretender too which I learned well to be as a teenager. I just carried those "tricks" into my disease. It didn't always work, but it got me through some bad days. When my kids got older and started all their activities. I was a major "baseball" mom. Looking back, I have no idea how I managed all of that. I guess being young and stubborn got me through. I also realize now reading so many threads about people that don't receive the medical help I did. I never had to find a doctor to validate my disease. I was fortunate that I found him so quickly, so I guess that was where I was able to manage my symptoms. If a med didn't work, we would find another med and try that. My illness did progress, which is not very common. My feelings are that it was from going through DMSO treatments. I don't know, but I am fairly certain it was the 50% mixture. That was where I went wrong. Instead of listening to my body telling me that the treatment wasn't working. I trusted my doctor and nurse that other patients felt better after a "few" treatments. I happen to get worse. Maybe I was the first of his patients that didn't respond to that treatment. I don't know, but I feel that is what made my IC worse. I still lived my life knowing that there were many more people worse off than me, so I very rarely allowed myself to sink down into depression. I lived my life growing up pretending all the time (long story) so I guess I just used those same tools and pushed through whatever challenged me. I also had my faith that God was in control. Not that every day was perfect, we aren't promised that, but I knew that there was a plan. I have been blessed to be put in the hands of caring doctors, great friends and a supportive husband. I can't say that everyone in my life has been supportive, but again, I learned the tools to work through that. Even this site has been one of my tools. I know this sounds sappy, but for people that are worried about your future and are upset that they have lost the life they knew. Find a way to put yourself in the best place you can. Don't try to get through this alone. Don't give up on people that don't understand what you are going through. Some people can't handle it, or they are afraid to ask questions, etc. I know I had a time that I was upset that life was moving on without me. My kids were teenagers, I was very busy with my job, things I had agreed to do, etc. I had alot of people praying for me, sending me notes of encouragement and the wonderful phone calls. I realized that I didn't have "it all", but I had what I needed. These days, I definitely have more good days than bad. Nights still aren't great, but God gave me the ability to function on less sleep than most people can get by with. I have also learned that being everything to everyone was not necessary. I can be a friend, I can also need a friend and be thankful for what I have. Even though I can look more towards the future because I am feeling better, I also recognize that sometimes I won't feel well, and I just can't push myself and it's better to take a break than break down. Hugs, Tracey
Tracey... awesome post! Very inspiring!!! - Jill

contents: Living in the NOW.... Came across this 5 year old post while browsing last night and it improved my mood by about 1000%. Made me realize we only have the NOW to live in. If we fret about the past and future we miss the opportunity to appreciate what is good that is happening to us NOW. Happy Thanksgiving everyone. Bob Originally posted by ShePurzz Dear Megan Colleen, I just read your post and I hope that the highlighting above will help you to reorganize your thoughts and do a little 'proving' before you allow yourself the luxury of spending even just ONE MORE MINUTE in the Land of IF. There is a great exercise you can do to help counter this kind of thinking. Cognitive Thought Stopping is a great technique that you can try. Make yourself a big STOP SIGN and when you hear yourself start to wonder IF something is going to happen -- STOP -- and tell yourself that WHEN something happens you will deal with it -- but you won't allow yourself the luxury of worrying until it actually happens. Place stop signs in every room -- and when you hear your inner voice asking IF, look at the sign (sight) and say the word (sound) and you will help yourself to get a grip on this inner negative thinking. When you see the stop sign, redirect your focus to something pleasant, and say peace or relax to yourself... it will soon be happening more than the IF thinking will. Another really helpful suggestion is to PROVE your statements: When you worry about an IF situation: PROVE IT... for instance you wrote.. "If my case got worse, and if I lost my ability to work, it would be the worst thing that could happen to me..." To PROVE that statement you can take the result -- "...the worst thing that could happen to me" and PROVE THAT TO BE TRUE!!! and start to try to prove it by putting on paper other things that you can think of that WOULD be worse --- for instance, I think that having an arm or a leg amputated would be worse than not being able to work or having a bad case of IC. I also think that losing my spouse or my child would be worse.. If fact, hearing that I had CANCER would be worse too for me... You try it... fill in the blanks when you tell yourself saying these worrisome thoughts... When you downsize that worry, you will be amazed that you will stop living in the Land of IF and start LIVING the life you are in without wasting so much time in the LAND that doesn't really exist anyplace but in your own mind. I sure hope this will help you to stop the worrying... PM if you would like more suggestions like this!!! HUGS, prayers and love, Mary

I find I have the most relief from symptoms when I'm a) taking a hot bath, and b) when it's "that time of the month." one thing that really makes my symptoms worse, on the other hand, is driving. I don't understand what about these situations is affecting my IC symptoms though...heat from the bath makes sense, but I've always heard that baths aren't very good for hygiene.. I'm really not sure why the period makes it better. And it's really frustrating that I always need a bathroom after being in a car. Any thoughts as to why my symptoms respond to things like this? I wonder if I can figure out just WHAT is helping or hurting in these cases, I can use the reasons to sustain relief when I'm not in the bath, or the other three weeks of the month and maybe help driving pains.. thanks for the advice

Most of us agree that hormone fluctuations can effect our IC. The warmth of the bath is probably what helps --- I don't do tub baths because of the risk of infection, but some ICers don't have that problem. If it helps, I say go for it. Riding in a car can be a problem for me because of the vibration, even in a smooth riding car. Sitting on my IC pillow helps. Donna

contents: I find riding in a car really bugs my bladder, so I place a heating pad on my belly cover it with a pillow, for some reason the pillow gives added comfort nad then put seat belt around all of it. I use a regular heating pad and bought an inverter that I plug into my cig lighter and then it has a normal spot to plug heating pad into. I find heat helps me alot. I also have muscle relaxers for long car rides. MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: A little trick that works for seatbelts. If you pull the shoulder/lap belt out so it's very loose, then put a spring clothespin on the belt against the top of it, the clothespin will keep the belt from tightening. If you have an accident, the belt will tighten and the clothespin will pop off. (kind of hard to explain) I do this for long drives. Donna

contents: Thanks Donna for posting this trick! I've been having a heck of a time trying to keep the seat belt loose with one hand and drive with the other. Pam

Hello all! I was just curious. Since there are several steps given to help reduce a flare as soon as you feel one coming on, are there any of you who eat/drink things you know you can't have and right after start on these things? I mean the Prelief, the Baking Soda in water, the Tums, even a pain med and a Pyridium? I am just curious as to the success rate that would have. Obviously you wouldn't do this multiple times a day and throughout each week. But if like your family is going to a Mexican restaurant for someone's birthday and you really want to be able to eat one of your no-no menu items there etc. Would you feel able to eat that or those things and come home right away and do all the 'coping with flares' techniques with success?

For me, it doesn't work. I tried in the beginning, to eat things along with Prelief, but I still had the flare and the interruption of quality of life that happens with it. For me, it's just not worth it and I don't think I'd even enjoy the food now, knowing from painful experience that I can be sick for several days afterwards. I wish I had a better answer for you, but for me, that's kind of just how it is.

Apparently God thinks I'm a bad-ass" ~Author Unknown Source - Pinterest " Current treatments: -IC diet -Elavil 50mg at night -Continuous use birth control pills (4-5 periods/year) -Heparin/Marcaine/Sodium Bicarb home instills at night 3-4x per week, more often if needed -Pyridium if needed, -Pain medicine at bedtime daily, as needed during the day several times per week -Antibiotic when doing an instillation to prevent UTI -Colace & SmartFiber to treat chronic constipation from meds, Fleet enema as needed -Dye Free Benadryl 50 mg at bedtime -"Your Pace Yoga: Relieving Pelvic Pain" dvd, walking, treadmill at gym -Managing stress= VERY important! -Fur therapy: Hugging the cat!

contents: That's a good point about pain. Since I don't experience pain the possibility probably sounds more appealing to me. The only thing I would be really worried about is loss of sleep for more than a few days. If I knew I would get frequency for like 3-5 hours and then be done with the flare I SO would take that choice. I am too chicken to try it yet. I have no idea as of yet how long my flare from that would last.

I've had diagnosed IC for 36 years and I still wouldn't try it. When I'm stuck in a Mexican restaurant, I will order either a crisp bean burrito (after asking exactly what's in it) or a plain hamburger --- most places will have hamburgers. Donna

I did not use the flare tactics immediatly after the coffee and i wonder if i did would it have stopped the pain. Im too sacred to try again.

contents: I hear you Claire. While I am very conscience about the need to try new things (so I don't end up scared to try anything not on the IC approved diet ever) it's still hard. At least for me when I flare I am triggering depression and anxiety from before I got my diagnosis and was in so much despair. I try to remember that if I flare now I have all the tools (meds) and knowledge I didn't back then to get me through it. It's still a struggle for me though.

That is a good question. I know in the beginning if I ate anything I wasn't sure of I would take a couple tums right after I ate out of paranoia so maybe it helped, but I guess I'll never know. I never had the nerve to try something like red wine or regular coffee just to experiment because I know now what it does to me.

Hello all, I was diagnosed a month ago and only showed symptoms a month before that (I know I am quite lucky.) So this is the 3rd week I have been on the IC diet (strict - only maybe food I have had was avocado and root beer barrels but I didn't get a flare from either). My flares come about 3 hours after I eat something bad and last for a few hours. I am on Vesicare, Elmiron, Elavil, Allegra, Pyridium and Nucynta for pain. I had a Cystoscopy 2 weeks ago to re-confirm my diagnosis. Since Monday I have not had any significant pain. IE, I wouldn't even have to take ANY form of medication for pain; not even Tylenol. BUT. I am getting the oddest symptoms from noon-4pm. I feel like I have a fever (but I don't). And my frequency increases. It's not even like a flare for me at all. It's not a flare frequency but it's just like I drank a lot of water and have to pee a lot. Also if I stand up I feel like my head is tilted (the room looks tilted). I am not dizzy, it's just everything's not straight. Through trial and error I have discovered if I take half of a pain pill (Nucynta) it goes away. The fever feeling, the tilted room, and the frequency. I told my doctor about this so he tested for infections (since I had the cystoscopy I am guessing) and like he thought there's no infection at all. (I was on a week of antibiotics after the Cysto too). He can't give me an explanation as to why I feel like this. He basically said, "That's what the Nucynta is for and if I am not afraid of you being on it for a while you shouldn't be either." But I am. I don't want to stay on a narcotic for longer than I need to. I know Nucynta is less habit forming and doesn't screw with your head as much as others out there but still. Any suggestions as to why this is happening? It basically feels like the flu every afternoon for me. I have tried drinking more water (2 is what my norm is; I know this is low for some people but I have a lower tolerance for water (Yes I am a medical freakshow heh) and I work from home so I don't need as much as I did say when I was in school). But I have tried drinking 5-8 a day just to see if it was that. It only made me further ill. Basically I am at a loss as to what to do. I work in the afternoons and I can barely concentrate because of this. How long did your first 'flare' last? I feel like I have 2 kinds of flares. The horrible ones (like when I drank a Red Bull last month) and I hits me for 3 hours hardcore. And then a 'constant' little flare like I am still having even with the meds/diet. At this point I am kind of just depressed. I think it's mostly because in the span of 2 months I went from totally healthy to totally not healthy. I didn't even get the chance to have like my favorite last meals before I had them ripped away from me. I have lost 10 pounds in my diet weeks just because almost all the 'good diet foods' happen to be ones I hate. So it's a lot of oatmeal, PB, chicken, potatoes and noodles, and cucumbers for me. How long did it take you guys to 'mourn' your old food My mom (bless her) is trying to make me some OK recipes like jack mac and cheese, and red bell pepper sauces (to substitute tomato) and cottage cheese ranch etc. But since I hate jack cheese and peppers and cottage cheese to begin with I am not liking these at all. My doctor thinks that my IC (ulcers/sores) came from Red Bull mostly. I was a 1-3 a day drinker. I like the taste (it stopped giving me energy years ago) so I was drinking those in substitute for sodas. Basically my cysto showed that when my bladder stretches my bladder lining does not - it rips apart hence my sores. And that because of the Red Bull I can't even have spicy foods now because my bladder lining is pretty non-existent. He told me I could probably even have any food I wanted with some prelief (minus caffeine, spicy foods, and alcohol) but I am scared to try. I wanted to get this long flare over with first. *sigh* Sorry this is so long. I am just so desperate. Last edited by nineteenwinters; 08-10-2011, 11:22 AM.

Maybe it's from the combination of meds you are taking. I'm very medication sensitive and I used to get sick to my stomach from just pyridium. The OAB meds also made me feel ill and sometimes I would break out in a sweat. I think that drs. are so used to prescribing a combo of meds that they fail to realize not all people can handle it. Prosed was one med that made me feel like the room turned- not really dizzy but just like everything moved suddenly. A really strange feeling. My flares were all different. In the beginning when it started it was one long 4 month flare and afterward before I got serious about the diet they would last a few days to a few weeks. After I got serious about the diet they were a few hours or maybe overnight. Now I don't hardly ever get one.

What you're experiencing could be because you were drinking a lot of a very high caffeine drink and your body may be addiction --- if that's the case, you should feel better in a week or so when your body is more accustomed to being without it. People go through the same kind of thing when they stop smoking. It's not fun. I hope you feel better soon. Warm hugs, Donna

contents: It could very well be the drugs. I just don't know which one(s) would be the cause. I am hesitant to stop taking any of them because I am so new to this. I would cry if I went back to the frequency I was before them. I think I'd rather have the 'flu' for a few hours than have to pee every 5 minutes. Maybe I'll be lucky if it is the pills and after my first month or so this problem will go away. It could also be the caffine. I did go off of it when I was 18 until I was 19. While I only had withdrawl symptoms for a week I wasn't drinking Red Bull it was just Dr. Pepper mostly. I really hope it is the caffine and not the pills. Thanks for the advice.

I had similar feelings for quit awhile, although not the head tilt, the only way I found to deal with it was to lie down and have a nap every afternoon, I think it was my bodies way of saying I needed a rest. In time this pasted unless I get over tired and then I will feel the same way again. Did you substitute the Red bull with anything sugary because if you did it can perk you up and then have a rebound effect of making you drop and if your sugar level goes ow you can feel this way. You could ask your Dr. to do a fasting blood sugar test on you, it is quick and easy. Good luck MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Hmm...I will have to try taking a nap during my lunch break. I have been sleeping a lot (11pm until 10am) so I thought I was getting plenty of rest but maybe not. I haven't had anything to drink but water and Chamomile tea once in a month. I did have my sugars tested 2-3 weeks ago to make sure I didn't have diabetes but maybe it was too soon. I hope mine passes like yours did. I am beginning to get depressed. If it's not a flare up its this. I wonder if ill ever have a normal life again.

Things do get better it takes time to get it all figured out. You maybe sleeping lots but sometimes it comes down to quality of sleep, this was my issue. Now that I don't eat sugar and my sugar levels don't swing up and down this tiredness is not such a problem for me. Be patient it takes lots of trail and error to get everything involved with IC figured out. MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Just thought I would reply since I finally figured out what was making me so yucky. It's the Nucynta. Apparently it only took me a weeks worth (after my Cysto pain) to get ridiculously addicted. Over the weekend I spent all day in bed trying to see if 'rest' would help. It didn't. I tried taking Tylenol to take the edge off and it did absolutely nothing. It was then that I realized what was happening. Sure enough I took a 1/2 of a Nucynta and it 30 minutes I went from bed ridden to 100% feeling great. So now I am just tappering off of it like I have done with Prozac and Clonazepam in the past. It's insane how addictive some things can be!

ughh yes some medications are so addictive!! I take klonopin and I dread the day when I will have to wean off it as well! As for the two types of flares, I get both like you do. A really bad one that lasts a few hours and a minor underlining one. I wish they would just go away! I do morn the loss of food as well. The month has been a crazy reality wake up call for me. I was first diagnosed with food allergies to wheat, dairy, eggs, beef, lamb and a few others.. then I went on IC diet. Basically I feel like I am eating in a retirement home at time. Not that there is anything wrong with a retirement home, but as you know the food is super bland and never seasoned. Pretty much what I eat. Oh well, I guess it could be a lot worse for me!
signature: -Pammylynn Rose Wishing everyone a pain free bladder day! Painful symptoms start in May 2011 Diagnosed with IC in Aug 2011 MEDICATIONS Elmiron Axert (only for rare migraines) Strict IC diet Gluten/dairy/egg free diet

contents: I had a really bad experience with Klonopin so I only took it a few days. I bet it's like Clonazepam is though and when you wean off it will take a few months. It's not fun but it is managable. Especially after living with IC! I totally agree about the food. I know some people on our diet really don't mind it but it's horrible to me. I miss flavor. The only time I get to eat something I feel has flavor is when I mix avocado with garlic. To me that's the closest thing I can get to 'spicy tasting'. I have taken to having a little of that every day but I know I am going to burn out eventually.

Exact same thing happened to me with Tramadol. I'm glad you figured it out. Took me only a week of being on it, and when I came off I got SEVERE withdrawals- which I hadn't gotten being on Vicoden or Percocet for much longer periods of time.
signature: Symtoms started July 2010. Severe pelvic floor pain only. 2 time PT graduate! In medical remission since August 2011; able to eat and drink anything I want currently. IC meds: 200 mg Elmiron in the morning 100 mg Elmiron @ night Macrobid after intercourse 03/11 07/11 01/12 If at first you don't succeed: 07/26/2013!

contents: Glad ur feeling better! I feel u on food thing im italian. I miss my morning coffee soooo much. Plus im chocoholic.....i got diagnosed in june....have been always prone to utis thought it was that and it turned into this. Soooo thankful i found this forum with so many caring people!! Hope ur still feeling better Kierd

Ugh, withrawels suck. It was weird for me I got my first UTI 2 months ago which set off my IC full force. And of course I was consuming massive ammounts of cranberry juice and craberry supplements. Ow.
lol................that is what I was doing too, sooooo hurty. Now yummy boring diet!! But if it keeps me from flares it is worth it.

Hi all, Recently there was an article geared towards RA patients and their loved ones called 12 Nice Things You Can Do For Someone In Pain. http://www.health.com/health/gallery...9189_1,00.html I sent the article to Jill and with the suggestion that we make our own list for the IC community and she agreed to the idea. I'd love some input from everyone! What things do you appreciate when you are in pain? Thank you! Jess

contents: A hug is very nice. When I don't feel good --- having someone cook dinner & load the dishwasher.

contents: support system I think for me understanding is the most important thing. Anyone who tries, to understand how I'm feeling can make my day easier. I agree with Donna that having someone make dinner is comforting. Having anyone do anything to help, without complaints, or having to be asked, is nice. It's the little acts of kindness that make not feeling well feel not so bad. A phone call from one of my children is wonderful. We don't have to talk about my IC, and as a matter of fact, I usually would just as soon not. It's the funny stories about grand kids, cats ,hubbies, etc. that can make the pain fade into the background for a while. Calls from friends, just to chat, are great too. Great thread, thanks for starting it. Laurie
Thank you both for your input, ladies. I really appreciate it! Hugs to you both!
signature: Mommy to 2 crazy, wonderful kids and wife to the most amazing man in the world!

contents: Just basic human compassion. Seems though thats a thing of the past.
signature: (\__/) (o.O ) (> < ) This is Bunny. He's on his way to world domination.

contents: I love my husbands backrubs when I'm washing the dishes. It really helps to relieve all that tension that builds up while your hurting.

Someone who understands me! Thankfully my whole family does in one way or another. With me right now, its been my back pain and after a full day at work (plus the hour car drive home) I just want to go to bed; which I usually do-get on my pjs and turn on the TV and just veg out. My kids are old enough to help out by cooking or cleaning so I am grateful for that.

I have a friend that when I'm in a really bad flare or after having a cystoscopy/hydrodistension/fulguration done phones me every day to see how I am doing and to chat. She lives 8000 kilometers from me - but still she phones to chat and just to be there when I need to vent or want to discuss new treatment options. I also have a friend who is very considerate and knows there are only certain things that I can eat - so she buys those things and make sure she has things for me to eat on hand - for when I visit. Both of these friends are more like my sisters than my friends. I am so thankful for them both! I am so thankful that God has provided me with such a wonderful support system - which comes from my two university friends! Blessings, Rebecca
signature: Current Meds: 1 Elmiron 100mg 3x a day 1 Cimetidine 300mg 2x a day 4 Gabapentin 300mg 3x a day (3600mg) 1 - 2 Atarax 25mg at bedtime 1 baclofen 10mg 3x a day as needed 200mg of Pyridium 3x a day (usually do in cycles - on for a week or two as needed) - I am very careful about this. 2 belladonna and opium suppositories as needed for pain daily. Bladder instillations (Lidocaine, Heparin, & Sodium Bicarbonate) I have tried but failed - lidocaine instillations, heparin instillations, oxybutynin by mouth also called ditropan, tylenol 3s with coedine, cyclobenzaphrine, Ativan, vesicare, Oxytrol patches worked for 3 to 4 years and then failed, I tried Uracyst and had some success but my urologist felt that it was not successful so we moved on to trying something else. . [/I]

contents: Doc just gave me a RX for Uribel. I am much more confortable. Cost of drug. Go to the Uribel web site and download coupon. My insurance does not cover this drug. With the coupon it was $99.00. It seems a lot but the results have been worth. it. I hope that my flare subsides and I don't have to use it too often. I still use Prelief. I hope this information helps those in need.

You might ask your pharmacist if there is another brand that is cheaper. There a quite a few of the "blue" drugs that mostly contain the same ingredients. Check your insurance formulary and see if you can find another one that is covered, I am sure your Dr would be willing to change your script. Those type of meds come and go frequently under different names from different companies, that is probably why it isn't on your formulary. I am glad that it is helping you. Sandra
