contents: I use UTA,another blue Urinary Tract Anticeptic.I don't know the cost of it as I only have a $9.00 co pay. It works well.

I would really really like to try one of the blue meds, but I was prescribed Hyoscyamine (one of the ingredients) separately and it made my frequency/urgency SO MUCH WORSE! Which is saying a lot, considering I was already going over 30 times a day...on Hyoscyamine, it increased my frequency to 65 times per day. It was really awful... I still am tempted to try the Uribel/Urelle/blue meds though, because I am really desperate for a solution.

contents: I saw my Dr. yesterday, i have had lots of trip into see him lately with one issue and another. He often gives me a shot of ketoralac, it is a NSAID but it doesn't usually both me. Yesterday he said I should take it with my other meds for a few days to see if things will settle down a bit. I knew better but I was not thinking I took it and 1/2 hour later my bladder was on fire, still is I have been up since 4 am, all my usual things haven't calmed it down. I even have a book that i write meds in that don't agree with me and it says plainly never take again. I guess I was having a blonde moment and took it, and now I pay for it. I guess i spend the day in front of the computer with my heating pad. MG

contents: what's in NSAID to bother you? Mothergoose - I'm just curious what you think is in the ibuprofen (is that what you took?). Your flare was so quick, so was it acid that would respond to baking soda or was it that an NSAID reduces prostaglandins which in many people is good but in some people is bad. Supposedly you could increase protaglandins by taking something like evening primrose oil or black current or borage oils. I wonder if that would help. You could ask doctor as these can thin blood a little I think. A thought.
signature: I got IC in 1970! I was not diagnosed until 1991. I've tried many drugs and therapies but I tend to only resort to drugs when in a flare because when I am not in a flare (from being good on diet), I suffer only from small bladder volume (like about 7 ozs.) and peeing will relieve the discomfort. When I am feeling relatively normal, I say to myself I am glad I am not on a drug. When I am in a flare, I say, why am I not on a drug! I've recently have been trying to solve my connective issue problems in general. I look to diet and herbs mostly unless it gets really bad. I still think there is great hope for each individual finding a path to healing and there are many.

contents: The NSAID I took was named Ketoralac (sp) need an rx for it. I had no idea there were differences to the NSAIDS, I just know they all pretty much bother me with the exception of Ponstan/Ponstal. Last year there there was a manufactor shortage of Ponstan and I tried most other NSAIDS that my Dr. could RX me and they all bothered my bladder. It pretty much has settled down today, but that was 4 days, from one pill, won't do that again. MG thank you for your help MG I was not an ibuprofen , if something is going to bother my bladder it ca do it within 20 mins. Thanks for the suggestions I am very med sensetive and allergic to herbs and spices so I probalby can't take borage oils, and I already know I can't take black current. As for Evening Prime rose oil I am not too sure about.
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Does anyone flare up during pollen season? I remember a guest lecturer discussing this once; it's now allergy season here in Southern California and I'm noticing a slight increase in bladder symptoms to go along with my sneezing. Have been under stress lately too, so hoping that's it rather than looking forward to an all-spring-long-flare...

It’s not uncommon for IC patients to have IC symptoms worsen under stress; nor is it uncommon for IC patients to become or sensitive to foods, pollen, etc. Also, stress is known to lower our immune system. So maybe it’s just the onset of stress that is making your bladder working a little more than usual. That way you have a great spring season. Have and great St. Paddy's Day!
signature: Kat

contents: Many ICers find that their nasel allergies also hurt that bladder. I am also affected by major weather changes. Sometimes it is more than one thing that causes a flare...like if you weren't stressed maybe the polen wouldn't bother you...and the stress alone may not be enough to bother you...but both together were enough. Flares may be because of: stress, food, food allergies, nasal allergies, Enviromental hazzards, over working yourself, sex, gas, constipation, holding it too long, hormones, yeast infections, bacterial infections, car rides, viruses or being sick, being over tired, treatments affecting you badly, test affecting you badly, and many other things. Some things you can help lesson or prevent…some not.

He agreed that he has had a lot more complaints. I also noticed that I seem more swollen. It was a lot harder to do my pfd exercises and my joints have all swollen and become achey. I was glad to see a fellow orange county resident on the boards! I'm from San Juan Capistrano, my doctor is in Mission Viejo. Hope to talk to you again soon.
signature: Jenny Things turn out best for people who make the best of the way things turn out. - John Wooden ---------------------------------------------------- IC, IBS, PFD, CFS-FM, PCOS, RLS, TMJ Wife, stay at home mother of two beautiful girls, former art teacher and architect

contents: Pollen related to increase in IC symptoms? I've been feeling pretty good and pain free for quite some time, but yesterday a new flare started and it's still going. It's getting close to springtime here in Norway and that also means pollen season. I've thought about the possibility of pollen affecting IC symptoms before, but I just never really thought it possible. Now I'm starting to think it is! Every year, this time of year my pain increases and lasts pretty much all spring/summer.. I have been tested for allergies, but the tests have come up negative. I have no known allergies, but I still experience an increase in pain during this time of year. I'm starting to miss winter already Does anyone else share this experience?

Hi, Diane57 ~ This is interesting you brought up allergies. I live in Corona and I've started sneezing and itchy eyes pretty bad and my IC symptoms are acting up too. I never connected the two. I do take a daily Claritin and was hoping it would ward off the sneezies! I never had allergies until the last few years....hmmm, also began the IC symptoms about then too. Hope we're all feeling better real soon.
signature: Never heard of IC til suspected....Never knew so many suffer from it. Praying for a cure. I've found answers...so can you...keep the hope. 11/2007 IC Symptoms began during recovery of surgery for bladder susp & hysto: painful/urge/freq urination up to 50 times a day, pain walking, even water hurts. 8/2009 Susp IC; 1/2010 Treatment began; 2/2010 Diag PFD, PT, Surg remove mesh; 5/2010 Surg, 2nd bladder repair, vag vault/sm int prolapse repair, IC confirmed in surg; 7/2010 Diag Candida Esophagitis, Gastritis, Diviticulosis, Gallstones, 8/2010 Surg gallbladder TREATMENTS (updated 4/15) IC Diet since 8/2009 (Able to vary 4/15) Heparin/Lidocaine/Bicarb/Kenalog Instills 1xDay Aloe Vera Capsules, (2) 2xDay, Loratadine 1xDay Pantoprazole, Prelief, Pyridium up to 3xDay, Premarin Cream 3xWeek *DISCONTINUED since feeling better: Celexa 10mg daily*, Fiber supp*, Gabapentin 12/2011 worked well *, Macrodantin 100mg after instill*, Probiotics*, PT-PFD*, Valium Vag Suppositories: Cyclo/diaz/lido, 10/5/62.5mg * PROB MEDS: Elavil, up to 50mg 1/2011 ret&hbp, Urelle ret, Vesicare ret

contents: Yes, yes, yes! My IC is always brought on by outside pollen. I have gotten to the point of hating Spring and Summer because it means pain for me. During the winter I can eat and drink whatever I want and not flare. But during allergy season, my bladder is very sensitive to everything. I hate it. It's hard to enjoy the sunshine and budding trees and flowers when it brings on flares!

Allergy IC My IC is really bad right now due to allergies. I was taking Allergy drops instead of shots last year, but that sent me into a huge flare. So, I am switching to allergy shots. Since most allergy meds cause retention, I am presently using an antihistamine and cortisone nasal spray, and it has been working quite well. I hate this time of the year. I used to look forward to springtime, not anymore. I need to move to Hawaii.
signature: I have IC, Fibromyalgia, Factor V Leiden, Infertility, Asthma, Allergies, early menopause. Medications: Elavil 100 mg Cystoprotek Desert Harvest Aloe Elmiron 600mg/day Morphine Heparin injections Rescue Instillments: Heparin, lidocaine, gentamycin, sodium bicarbonate 2x/week.

contents: I live in California and, like you, suffer from allergies and IC flare-up. I've learned that Claritin eases both my allergies and IC symptoms. Likewise, certain food that trigger histamines cause me to sneeze and feel uncomfortable IC symptons. For me, it's walnuts and wheat so I am on a gluten free diet and avoid walnuts and feel great. I think there is definite relationship between histamines and IC for me.

I too have flared every spring for the last 3 years like clockwork. This time is horrendous. Never had a flare like this and it is going strong after 5 weeks. The pollen is everywhere and I am a very allergic person. The ironic thing is I have no respitory allergy in spring anymore and I always used too before IC. WTH??? I took Zyrtec last year but it made me retain. Dose claritin make you retain? I did not see that side effect on their website. Would trying atarax help? Benydryl before bed does not work. I have never tried atarax adn a worried about being very sleepy.

I have never correlated it before...but I am worse in the spring!!! WOW!! I take atarax at night...but its true, every spring my symptoms do get worse...so far I have not had a flare this spring like in the past, but my "nagging" pain/symptoms have increased. I also have seasonal allergies, but never connected them to my IC...learn something new everyday!!!!
Yes, I flare EVERY year around this time! Something about spring. I don't have a problem with other seanson changes, just spring. Stress is MY #1 cause for flares!

contents: Claritin at night helps me sleep and control allergies somewhat. However...... I am also looking at why I have allergies. Like you, I am chemically sensitive and seem to have food allergies. Histamine producing foods trigger pelvic discomfort for me so I have been eliminating them one by one to find the ones that cause me to have congestion AND pelvic pain. So far I have eliminated red wine and walnuts and gluten. For one month I am eating only vegetables, fruit, and lean meat right now and have NO symptoms of any discomfort anywhere. I'll add back one food at a time to see how I react after a month. The acid-free diet does nothing for me. I read a book recently on gluten-sensitivity (not celiac disease) and it started me on a discovery path that seems to be working. Could you have a food allergy? There is a home kit ALKAN that you send a blood sample off to a lab that tests for food allergies for you or you can do it "the hard way" like I am. I also have fibromyalgia, restless leg syndrome, and crazy heart muscle spasms that I am hoping are all related to food sensitivitis. So far, so good! Good luck and keep searching!!!

I'm so glad I came across this thread. I've been going crazy all week with a really bad flare. (I've been practically symptom free for a long time) but I realized that I went to my Uro last June for a flare and found an old post of mine here for June of 2009. So for sure, there is something in May/June triggering it I would think and with all your comments you kinda confirmed it. I have a stash of Atarax so I am gonna start that tonight. Hopefully it helps bc it's been bad. Question though - I don't have any "normal" allergy issues like sneezing or sore throat or itchy eyes, etc. Could it still be affecting my bladder this way? It's just so strange to me. Thx
signature: Diagnosed: 07/11/07 after Cystoscopy - mild case of IC, I also have PCOS. Medicines: Elavil 25 mg - 2 x a day Elmiron 100 mg/3 x a day Macrobid - after sexytime Pyridium - as needed - it helps! metformin - for PCOS Stopped taking: Detrol LA 5 mg and Vesicare 10mg

I guess caffeine is off the limits forever back to drinking that marshmallow tea...grr...no energy from that.

contents: I did the same thing with Starbuck's coffee. After my two months of pt work and feeling like I was normal again I went and had Starbuck's coffee a few days in a row. I was feeling like I needed a lift, but boy was I sorry. I've heard that Starbuck's coffee is the most acidic coffee out there due to how many times it is roasted- which I guess gives it the robust flavor but sure adds the acidity. I've also heard that cold pressed coffee (which you can make at home from beans) takes out the acid. Not absolutely sure how much acid it takes out, but if anyone out there knows it would be good to hear.

contents: Hi Anna, this is totally off subject, but I notice your name Luvterriers. I had a rat terrier, Bogart,up until last November when he passed away. I loved that little dog so much! What a great personality he had. We got him at a rescue place and someday soon, we will look for another.

Retention is a huge problem When IC first started for me I had frequency like crazy and moderate pain. Now it seems like every medication I try causes me to STOP urinating. Pain medication (my pain is now very severe), anti-depressants, and it seems foods. Also, I am becoming MORE sensitive to things I could take in the past. Does anyone have any suggestions? I can take NOTHING for constipation without severe bladder pain. I can't take anything for GERD because of severe bladder pain. If I take more pain medication to help with the pain I can't pee. Please, does anyone have any suggestions? What do you do when you've tried all the conventional treatments and nothing works ? I NEED an anti-depressant, but they ALL interact with the one pain med I am currently taking (but thinking of going off of) that I'm not allergic to (I'm allergic to morphine, fentynal, percocet). I throw up, break out in hives, etc. I need a pain med and an anti-depressant has anyone had this problem? Help....
signature: Teresa We are not HUMAN BEINGS going through a temporary SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE. We are SPIRITUAL BEINGS going through a temporary HUMAN EXPERIENCE....so that we may become more SPIRITUAL.

contents: Snowden1 - Sorry to hear about your problems, but what I think you need is a low dose Klonopin 0.5 mg twice a day to calm down your bladder. I'd also suggest drinking marshmallow tea since it's soothing to irritated tissues. Are you taking any nutritional supplements? PM me if you'd like some suggestions. I hear ya about the anti-depressant, I went through the entire line of SSRI's -it was terrible, until I finally setlled with Wellbutrin XL. As far as foods...is there anything that doesn't cause you pain? If so, stick with eating just that for a while. Hugs

contents: You didn't mention what class of antidepressants you've used. I couldn't take any of the tricycic antidepressants, but I could take the other class that includes Prozac. All the tricyclics ( elavil,tofranil, pamelor etc.) would cause me some retention and nervousness, but Prozac never did.

425runner, Thanks for the response. I should probably post my meds at the bottom of my page. I take Klonapin .5mg twice a day. I also take all Hormones, (subq) for complete hysterectomy that started this mess three years ago. Exalgo (this is extended release Diluadid) 8 mg once a day and Diluadid 2 mg for breakthrough pain. I can only eat organic chicken (a few other meats), rice, rice milk, canola oil, olive oil, lettuce, almonds, organic rice cereal, green beans, organic cane juice, and potatoes. This is my list and this is really all I can eat. I have tried to add other things in and I know eggs cause pain, dairy causes pain, vitamins and minerals like Vit C, B and all magnesium. I am gluten intollerant so no bread and most of the gluten free has egg whites or something I can't have. When this first started I could eat more. Now it is much worse. Aloe Vera was helping for awhile a long time ago and then I started breaking out in a rash. I have since tried to take it again and had the same results. I am not sure about Marshmellow root tea. I used to drink it in the tinture without alcohol just in warm water. It did increase my frequency,, not sure if it helped with the pain because there was so much trying different foods or meds all the time. I have some and can try it again. I need to make sure my bladder is somewhat calm or I can't tell if something is making it worse. I will try it again by itself thanks for the reminder about it. I seriously don't go off the IC diet or my crazy diet I call it at all. Never. It hurts way too much. For over 2 years the only thing I did go off for was blueberries and I paid for that.

contents: earthlady, I have tried mostly the SSRI's I think Lexapro, Paxil (was on this 17 years before getting IC) Prozac, Cymbalta (terrible retention), Prestique (spelling?), Well butrin, Elavil, some others too I just forget right now. Lexapro really worked well for me until they gave me Diluadid for pain. It interacts in a dangerous way for me. I throw up, get dizzy, feel faint, get a rash, really, can't handle light or sound, it is really bad. Lexapro before the Diluadid was really helpful and stopped my frequency. Now I can't get off the Diluadid and if I could I don't know onto what. I am allergic to percocet (can't breathe), Fentynl (severe muscles spasms in chest), morphine (stops urination, jittery), subqtex (spelling - severe vomitting), Lyrica (did nothing) caused blurry vision, Gabapentin -2500 mg began having stomach issues and so sleepy. Can't remember there were some others.

contents: Hi, On Monday I am part of a 1.5 hour conference panel presentation. There are 6 people on the panel (all my coworkers). I am the first speaker and will be giving about a 20 minute presentation. After that I will be "facilitating" the transition from one speaker to the next. I should be able to "hold it" for 1.5 hours if I don't get nervous and don't trigger a flare. I would prefer not to leave the panel as that would look a bit "odd". However, I might need to. Does anyone have any suggestions for how I could leave the panel discretely to use the washroom without having people raise their eyebrows? Thanks.

If you have a pager or cell phone, you could pretend it's a vibrator instead of ring tone; reach into a pocket and look at the pager or phone, then quietly leave for a few minutes. Nobody will think anything of it. Sometimes a little deception can save the day --- and just having a plan in place just might keep you calm enough for the whole session without a break. Donna

contents: Thanks for the suggestion Donna. Yes, having a plan in place will make me feel better I am sure. I was also thinking of asking people to take a couple of minutes to stretch (since it's a long presentation and then run to the washroom), but I don't want people to leave the room and not come back to hear the second half of the presentation).

I haven't been on the boards for such a long time. I was doing so well for the past six months. I could eat anything that I wanted except for tomatoes and soft drinks. I didn't have any pain at all so I went off the elmiron due to thinking it was causing stomach problems. Well now I am hurting and burning in my urethra. I feel like I am having so much pressure on my urethra. I have been peeing constantly. I went to the doctor, but no infection. They gave me cipro anyway and gave me some kind of overactive bladder pills. I did a treatment, but they are not working. (lidocane, sodium bicarb, and heparin. The only thing I have been eating that I usually don't eat is cheese and vanilla ice cream. Could cheese cause problems? It is just american cheese. I feel like it is PFD, but nothing is helping. Any suggestions? thanks, Maryann

How long ago did you stop elmiron? Most people who are helped by it do keep taking it, sometimes at reduced dosage. It can take a while for symptoms to return after you stop taking it. Have you tried emptying the elmiron tablet into water (discard the empty capsule) and taking it that way --- some people find it's the capsule itself that is the problem. I think it would be a good idea to get back on the IC diet as well. I hope you feel better soon. Donna

contents: Hi,Maryann ~ I'm sorry you're back into a painful cycle...I don't have any experience with Elmiron. I understand urethral pressure as that is one of my main symptoms. Have you been able to try Pyridium? It makes me sick to my stomach unless I eat something first, but seems to ease up some of that spasm type pain when I "go". I also understand eating just about anything you want. I'm in the same situation as you, enjoyed eating many of the "forbiddens" but slowly those horrible symptoms have crept back into my life.....even the frequency at night is bothersome again. Also, my trusted friend, my rice bag heated in the micro works wonders. It seems to relax down there enough that I even fall asleep. The "hope" is that you and I both had relief at some point so we just have to get back to our routine that WORKS. I've also been contemplating instills again, but first want to try the Pyridium. My doc has given me an Rx for 3xDay for a month to keep on hand....I've done that in the past due to the tearful agony but am now just trying as needed to see if my IC diet will get me out of this cycle. So far, I can feel some relief, but pressure returns. I'm really terrible at being able to "pinpoint" the cause of my problems so I don't have an answer about the cheese. I have the most success with the "strict" IC diet. I would think almost anything can cause this beast to rear it's ugly head however. I'm sure you'll get things under control. In the meantime, take good care of yourself and truly watch your diet. You must have been doing something very RIGHT to have felt so good for six months. Blessings,
signature: Never heard of IC til suspected....Never knew so many suffer from it. Praying for a cure. I've found answers...so can you...keep the hope. 11/2007 IC Symptoms began during recovery of surgery for bladder susp & hysto: painful/urge/freq urination up to 50 times a day, pain walking, even water hurts. 8/2009 Susp IC; 1/2010 Treatment began; 2/2010 Diag PFD, PT, Surg remove mesh; 5/2010 Surg, 2nd bladder repair, vag vault/sm int prolapse repair, IC confirmed in surg; 7/2010 Diag Candida Esophagitis, Gastritis, Diviticulosis, Gallstones, 8/2010 Surg gallbladder TREATMENTS (updated 4/15) IC Diet since 8/2009 (Able to vary 4/15) Heparin/Lidocaine/Bicarb/Kenalog Instills 1xDay Aloe Vera Capsules, (2) 2xDay, Loratadine 1xDay Pantoprazole, Prelief, Pyridium up to 3xDay, Premarin Cream 3xWeek *DISCONTINUED since feeling better: Celexa 10mg daily*, Fiber supp*, Gabapentin 12/2011 worked well *, Macrodantin 100mg after instill*, Probiotics*, PT-PFD*, Valium Vag Suppositories: Cyclo/diaz/lido, 10/5/62.5mg * PROB MEDS: Elavil, up to 50mg 1/2011 ret&hbp, Urelle ret, Vesicare ret

contents: American cheese does often have citric acid in it and colorants, so you might stop that for awhile and see if it helps. Sometimes I can get away with a slice on a burger, but not much more than that. And as Donna suggested, if you had good results on Elmiron, there may be another solution to the nausea than stopping it. Wishing you relief soon,

contents: Thanks everyone for the suggestions. I really appreciate it!

I had a flare (from stawberries and coffee mostly). I held that acidy stuff in and wow it built up until I thought it could almost be a UTI (but not really burning while urinating is a indication it wasn't). Anyway, sorry to be graphic, but some self PT was in order. Stuck thumb up vag. found a tender point just at entrance and pressed and it hurt. Pressed for a minute. OK that created its own pain and soreness but my bladder/urethra did feel better right after significantly with still a slight soreness from pressing, but it definitely helped.I'm not talking major PT. Can anyone try this and see if it works for you too? This would most likely not work on a long chronic flare, but I don't know.
signature: I got IC in 1970! I was not diagnosed until 1991. I've tried many drugs and therapies but I tend to only resort to drugs when in a flare because when I am not in a flare (from being good on diet), I suffer only from small bladder volume (like about 7 ozs.) and peeing will relieve the discomfort. When I am feeling relatively normal, I say to myself I am glad I am not on a drug. When I am in a flare, I say, why am I not on a drug! I've recently have been trying to solve my connective issue problems in general. I look to diet and herbs mostly unless it gets really bad. I still think there is great hope for each individual finding a path to healing and there are many.

contents: Hi All----- SO I super flaired 2 weeks ago, the lay in bed with heat, ice and drugs for 2 days, taking days to feel like yourself when you know it isn't over..the following week.....I had to fly (flairing) x 2 to a conference for work ON PAIN MANAGMENT! The only good thing as I had to duck out twice is when I said "flair plan" to my coworkers, they actually got it! Bad thing, trying to implement a flair plane on the plane on the way home. I decided against cathing (esp in a grubby airplane) so homemade ice packs in my crotch....that melted.....had to toss the undies and all. I made it home. Wed, flaired but laughing at the absurdity of it all. Hope that makes someone feel a little less alone (and vagina ice to my urethra is my savior for urethra spasms and heat for my belly)
signature: I am blessed to have an Indiana Pouch now IC since childhood, finally diagnosed at 31 yo PFD/Pelvic Pain,SI joint and LBP Low Back Pain s/p spinal fusion at 19 with more nastiness in back Hydrodistension 2001, 2005, 2011 w capsacian, 2011 with botox/ super pubic tube Interstim trial and removel 2002,C-Section 2007 and 2010, Post Partum Depression, post partum hypertension, Idopathic hypothalmic amenorrhea Radical Cystectomy with Indiana Pouch 2/3/12--->multiple complications and revision of ureters in pouch due to a giant ball of scar tissue in 8/12, occasional pylo, L kidney small and scarred, low flow Hysterectomy 2/3/12 w one ovary removed Dry Eyes L radical nephrectomy 1//3/14 after a long "what if " the conversation, kidney working 4 %, path said full of absesses Still have issues, still take meds. I have worked some, been doing pilates, just got a post masters in nursing, learning to pace myself. Learning things I can't do, learning things that I need to take a LONG time to master and regain myself, but mostly just so happy to be bladder free.:

contents: Good story Mel, and it's great that your co-workers "got it." You gotta keep laughing, crying gets you NOWHERE. Thanks for that, Bob appreciated it, he's always making those twisted tongue verbal errors, I chuckle, but everyone else looks at him like he belongs in the bin. Jill, wife of Bob

awww that sounds just like me. I was recently diagnosed in January this year. Some days I think I am losing my mind with all of the meds I am on. My doctor knows nothing about IC and I can't afford to keep going to my urologist. Some days seem hopeless, but I just keep trying different stuff. contents: I think with IC there are all ranges of "normal", lol. I can remember waking up and feeling great - only to start going downhill shortly thereafter. Also, for me nighttime always seemed worse because things "quieted" down and I had more time to think about what I was feeling, if that makes sense, lol. And even if I have a cold or flu, I always feel worse at night. I've always thought that probably the body is starting to tire out and can't "defend" itself as well or something. But if it's something that's just started or you are really concerned about it, it's always a good idea to talk to your doc!

signature: I am happy to say that I am off all pain medication and currently take Neurontin and Uribel for my IC. contents: I have some suggestions: Wear something comfortable for the drive; take along several small pillows so you can change positions; be sure to stop at rest stops on the highway, even if you don't feel you need to go yet; avoid any and all problem foods. contents: Teresa, I have to be honest I worry about it too. But I had a long talk with my pain doc, and he told me he would never prescribe more than I need for longer than I need. And right now, just to function around my house, I need some pain meds. He also told me that he would not just drop me and say Thats it, you're done. And make me go off cold turkey. Basically, I trust him. I know that this is what he's trained to do and so I take the medication. I don't always take as much as is prescribed. And some days I take every pill for that day. I just know that when I tried to cope with this on my own, I spent a LOT of time crying. Which I truly believe made my pain worse. Whatever you decide to do, make sure you're doing it in your best interest. Just because it doesn't kill you, it doesn't necessarily make you stronger. Take care of yourself.

contents: What kind of pain medicine is the doc prescribing?

Snowden1 For a urologist to say that just underscores their lack of understanding as to how debilitating IC can be. They either do not remotely comprehend what this disease does or they are afraid of liability. This disease and the pain it can cause can devastate and destroy a life as much or more than a drug might destroy a drug addicts life. I can say without hesitation that pain meds saved my life. For over 3yrs I had marginal pain control and lived each day wishing it was the last. I worked less and less, did less and less, spent more and more trying to find anything to help and basically became consumed by the constant pain. Now I have good pain control for the last few months and it is like being on a vacation from the hell I was in. Now I get things done, I work hard, I exercise and take care of my body and actually feel like I might have some real healing happening by being out of that cycle of pain and despair. I had frequency under control for a long while now and now with pain under good control I am almost normal, I can almost forget I have IC sometimes. It is good to have concerns and respect for opioid pain medications because that helps to ensure that they are used for what they are meant for. After long term use it is likely to have some physical dependence but that is a medical condition that can be treated like any other. When the time comes that you no longer need the meds you and your doctor plan a managed reduction and cessation. I wish you the best of luck and hope the new treatment allows you to live better, happier, fuller. Mike
signature: - Symptoms: started July 2007 New pain medication regimen has things under pretty good control for many weeks now. Exercise and eating healthy along with pain control have given me new life. Current Symptoms: pain in testicles, penis and general groin area. Other discomfort that is beyond pain, a maddening crazed feeling that defies description. New pain medication has most symptoms under good control. Frequency and urgency was a problem the first year or so but frequency is normal now. Diagnosed: Prostatitis first then IC by Cysto/Hydro Current Meds: Hydroxyzine 25mg 3 x day, Pyridium 200mg 3 x day (off 3-4 days on 3-4 days), Oxycodone, Norvasc (BP). Past medications and treatments: Protatitis and IC diet strict for 6 months, no change. Still watch diet although nothing seems to help or make worse. Have tried reverse, ate lemons to see if worse but no change at all. Meds tried - Amitriptyline, Detrol, Flomax, Cipro, Doxycycline. many other antibiotics, Uroxatrol, Vicoprofen, Elmiron (6 months at $320 mo nothing), Aloe, Cysprotek, Glucosamine, Marshmallow, various oils, tons more stuff to strange and numerous to recount. Hydrodistension for diagnostic was horrible for two weeks after I was sure I must be going to die because nothing could feel that way and let you live.

contents: Hey I know what you mean if some of them doctors could walk in our shoes for a day they would be putting themselves on the strongest pain med available. It is so sad to hear about so many doctors that do not understand or care about the excruiting pain we go through. they need to have more understanding and compassion.

Teresa, I am a Nurse Practitioner, I do have a few patients with IC, mostly Fibromyalgia, chronic low back pain. Having IC has certainly given me a different view of patients having chronic pain, but I still like them to be managed by a Pain Clinic since that is what they do best. Suzanne

aleet7, Yes, I understand not getting pain meds too. After more than 2 and a half years of asking all my doctors (9 plus) for pain meds, I finally found a doctor who understands that it is horrible living in chronic pain. Unfortunately, I have allergic reactions (a couple put me in the hospital) to many medications - especially pain meds. I am now trying to take Zofran to stop the severe nausea I get with this drug, but I haven't landed in the hospital yet (thank God). I just talked to the nurse today about some other side effects that were concerning me. Currently, this medication is not controlling the pain. I am going to try to see if my body can adjust to the side effects before quiting it or asking to move up on the dosage. I would recommend going to another doctor. I just kept seeing doctors and trying all IC treatments available until I really didn't have anything left to try. Suzanne, I bet having IC gives you a much stronger understanding for your patients and their need to be treated for pain. Do you also experience pain or more frequency/urgency?
signature: Teresa We are not HUMAN BEINGS going through a temporary SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE. We are SPIRITUAL BEINGS going through a temporary HUMAN EXPERIENCE....so that we may become more SPIRITUAL.

contents: Hi Teresa, In the beginning I had both frequency and pain( a lot of urethral pain). I started elavil and atarax right away which took care of the frequency. I have a military urologist who has been pretty good. He pretty much lets me try anything that I want. I'm on elmiron 500 mg a day, elavil 10-20 at night, atarax and cystoprotek. I did ptns with so-so results. I've been in PT intermittently for a year with great results. I tried Uracyst(8 instills) with no success. Did about 6 Elmiron instills with so-so results. I am now having some pretty good days. I've started taking Low Dose Naltrexone in Sept. I gave it to a patient with fibro, and Chrons. She came back 4 weeks later and said it was the best she felt in 1 year. I think my IC is due to an auto immune process. I also have thyroid disease , Diabetes and a high CRP( marker for inflammation, auto immune disease). Since starting the LDN I have felt so much better. I did have a flare last month from sex though. Sorry this is so long. I hope you find something for the pain. It is so draining to be in pain 24/7 and still have to carry on with your obligations. Suzanne

We too, were afraid of so much pain medication, but our NP assured us that if Bob no longer needed it, he would get him off of it. Don't forsee that anytime soon, but we are no longer scared of them, and they absolutely DO provide relief. If you need it, you need it. How can you live your life in constant pain? You get used to the meds, can function quite normally. Uros as a rule do not like to prescribe them (their job is to heal). So it is typical they will not. Good luck to you, and hope you find relief.

I cannot take tylenol because of allergies and alot of the narcotics have this in it... has anyone taken Demerol for bladder pain. I have tried oxydose(liquid form of oxycontin) and it gave me hives..also does anyone experience itching with narcotics???

I am allergic to demerol but often get itching and or hives with narcotics.. I would let your doctor know if you have either one. Good luck and hope your able to find a pain reliever that works for you. Happy holidays, Kelly

contents: Itching from narcotics is a quite common reaction; even though I don't get it all the time, I do get it sometimes, especially if I've been in a flare and have taken more than one or two pills of Percocet to try and control breakthrough pain. It even mentions it in the drug info my pharmacist provides with each med he fills for me, so it's not uncommon. Definitely discuss this with your physicians and your pharmacists; they can prescribe things that don't have the ingredients you're allergic to, and can also prescribe things to go along with your narcotics, such as antihistamines, that can help with that itchy scratchy reaction. As you've found out there are many pain relievers out there that do not contain Tylenol (acetominophen) -- Demerol is one, MS-Contin (morphine sulphate is another), and Oxycontin is a third. These last two are extended release, so you'd need to discuss a breakthrough painkiller with your doc; this is a med you can take when you get pain despite taking the long acting pill -- often happens when the pill is wearing off and the levels in your bloodstream are lower. Just have a heart-to-heart about available options with your doctor -- I feel you'll be able to find a combination of things that is just right for you!
signature: **** Jen *Diagnosed with severe IC in 2004 *Also diagnosed with PFD, fibromyalgia, chronic myofascial pain, IBS, migraines, allergies/asthma, dermatographism *Kept trying a million different treatments for all these things until I found what works, and I am doing okay these days with the help of a cocktail of medications and the InterStim, which was first placed in 2007. [I have had 2 revisions - one in 2010 when my battery died and had to be replaced, and one complete replacement (lead and generator) in 2012 after a fall on my stairs caused my lead to move.] *Current meds include Atarax (50mg at night), Lyrica (150mg twice a day), Xanax (0.5mg at night and as needed), Zanaflex (4mg at night), hydrocodone (10/325, every 6 hours as needed), Advair, Nasonex, Singulair (10mg at night), oral contraceptives, home instills containing Elmiron and Marcaine (as often as I need to do them). **I am not a medical authority nor do I offer definitive medical advice. I strongly encourage you to discuss your medical treatment with your personal medical care provider. Only they can, and should, give medical recommendations to you.

contents: Thank god for medical marijuana & hydrocodone. I wouldn't be able to function normally without them. My IC makes my back hurt, pelvis knifed, testicles slayed. I can't tell you how much it sucks to wake up every morning with a hurting pelvis & back, you get up and a few minutes walking around later your balls start to hurt from the back side under. The room lights up and you feel elevated, the pains horrible. Then the rest for the day it buzzes around and flares again and again >.< If I don't have me pain management I wouldn't get anything done.
Krecicki welcome to the board and thank you for that incredibly descriptive narrative about your pain. glad you get good relief thru your pain management.
I should have ben in pain back in school, I would have scored off the charts in english
if suffer we must, let's suffer on the heights. Thought you might appreciate Krecicki. It's Victor Hugo

I'm at my wits end. My daughter who is 12 has been doing really well with keeping the IC under control by taking her meds and following the IC diet. About a month ago the symptoms came back full force, causing her to miss two weeks of school. She made school today but only for half a day. Her main complaints are. pain in side, back spasms, urinary pain and cramps and leaking bladder. I took her to her IC dr two weeks ago and he wants her to see a pediatric urologist so the p.u. can perform a cystoscope and biopsy of the bladder. We saw him last monday and the procedure is scheduled for 12/16. Anyway, to make a long story short I am now out of ways to help get her through the next few weeks. Any suggestions that would relieve at least some of the pain/discomfort would be appreciated. Thanks!
signature: I am the mother of two daughters. One is 11 and was diagnosed last year with IC, the oldest daughter is now 14 and was diagnosed with IC also at the age of 11 but only after many months of suffering and misdiagnoses until we found Dr. Atkins in Wes Chester, PA who has been a huge support for Megan. You see most IC specialist will not see children, he made an exception and gave her her life back! She had a cystoscopy and hydrodistension in December 2010 which confirmed the diagnosis. Remember: You know yourself/your child. Don't let anyone convince you that "It's all in your/their head" or "you/they just don't want to go to work/school". Keep pushing until you get a doctor you are comfortable with and an answer that truly addresses the problems.

contents: If she can do her school work at home, it might be easier on her. That way she can have a heat pad. I know it seems like forever to her, but hopefully the time will fly by and the pediatric urologist will be able to help. Warm hugs, Donna

contents: I assume she has already been checked for an infection. A couple of other things come to mind. Her hormones might be kicking up and changing the way her symptoms manifest. Also, it wouldn't hurt to do some stress management activities with her. Look online with her and have her choose some things that she feels might help. That will encourage her to listen to her own body so that over time she can learn to soothe her symptoms. Three deep breaths, light stretching, warm baths in Epsom salts all come to mind. Finally, if she isn't keeping a journal that could be helpful in a number of ways. If she can get out, have her pick one out for herself..............Barnes and Noble and Borders have amazing ones. If she can't get out, Amazon has beautiful ones at well, and the anticipation of getting a package might just distract her. She can write whatever she likes.....not just her food and symptoms, but how she is feeling, how grateful she is for other things in her life, brainstorming ways to help herself. It doesn't matter much what she writes, just that she does it. Eventually the words will flow out. I hope this helps...............I feel so bad for her...............hugs hugs hugs.........

contents: Thanks Julie and Donna for all the great suggestions. Since we have tried just about all of the suggestions your replies at least make me feel a little bit better and not as useless. She has been home from school for the past two weeks and her teachers have been great with sending assignments home and decreasing her workload since she has extreme anxiety over missing the time. We are also working to get her homebound instruction for 4-8 weeks to get her through this time. She uses a biofeedback monitor that the therapist suggested along with deep breathing exercises. I have tried to get her to keep a journal, we actually talked about it last night. I'll let her know that someone else that suffers from IC suggested the journal and warm Epsom baths and maybe she'll do it then since it's not "mom telling her to do something". Thanks for the hugs I'll pass them along to Megan. Tracy

contents: Donna, Jill and other forum leaders, Is 12 years old too young to have their own little support forum on here? Perhaps it could be something that they could get into and not get in to any place else? Seems like at that age they may benefit, as was said, don't wanna do because MOM said so. Don't know if it is possible, but it was just a thought that you have probably already had.

I really don't have any suggestions about how to cope becasue I am 37 and cant barely cope with a flare. I can't believe she is only 12 and suffering with this. I definately think she should get home teching during this time because its tooo hard to leave the house. I usually have to lay on my side with a heating pad. Hot baths definately help with the pain if only for a little while. Hopefully after she sees the ped uro, they can find something to get it back under control. Poor baby : ( I feel so bad for her. Hugs and prayers.

It's a great idea to have a support forum for kids but it's also dicey because we want to make sure that parents are involved and supervising. There are also legal rules about age limits and since we do have discussions about sex, pain, etc... some of the content in the forum is not appropriate for a child to have access to... and it could also be frightening. That said, I will think about what we can do. Umm... did anyone mention using something like AZO bladder pain relief tablets or pyridium to help with the burning. Also, how about some pain control with pain medication... in children sized doses of course. What does the doc say about that?? I completely disagree with doctors who suggest that children don't experience pain. They sure do and I think it's important that they receive relief for that pain with medication if possible. Just my two cents.

contents: I agree with Jill abount content issue for kids.While Megan is involved with all the discussions with the doctor and knows what she has and the symptoms, side effects, what not. I'm trying to keep the issues she'll have to deal with later in life (her period, pain from sex, etc) away from her only because she is the type of child that would worry about it NOW. I think the current link for Children with IC works, this way parents can connect, share some information and then decide if they want the kids to connect through email. She is currently taking the AZO with the other IC meds and has a prescription for tylenol3. unfortunately the T3 gives her severe headaches. I called her dr (OB pelvic pain specialist) Friday and left a message on his nurses voicemail and today I called, spoke with the nurse in the am and then called again in the pm when I didn't hear back. Message I'm getting from nurse is that since she is a child and he is used to treating adults he's leary about increasing the medicines. Also, she has a scope and biopsy scheduled with a pediatric urologist but not until 12/16. Feel like I'm right back at start, have a 12yr old in severe pain laying on the couch crying that nobody believes her and that's why they are not calling her back. Actually thinking at this point of taking her to the ER for the pain, problem with that is the closest one to us doesn't really treat children and the closest children's hospital was the one we first went to that told us it was all in her head and she just didn't want to go to school. Getting very depressed that the holiday's are approaching and for the second time in two years I can't find anyone or anything to help so Megan's not going to be able to fully enjoy the holidays

signature: "Blindfolded fear does not lead to an awakening. Questioning with boldness does":woohoo: Diagnosed: Autoimmune Progesterone, P.F.D., Vulvodynia, IC.

contents: Something to think about She may be getting a hormonal thing going on. When I was young I had real bad PMS and I used progesterone cream natural.....Now after years of using it and stopping I am using it again and it is WAY helping with my symptoms. My understanding is that Estrogren can dominate our bodies. Using the progesterone cream can balance the bodies hormones. This is my second day on the cream and I have gone for peeing every half hour or less to every 3-4 hours!!!! Just something to consider. Also i drink lots of herb teas that are designed to heal the bladder.....it is working for me so far.... Jean Ann PS...this is all natural.....

Hubby is taking me to Punta Cana, Dominican Republic in two weeks....I am excited to go, but on the other hand i am a little nervous......flaring right now but am doing okay. Has anyone ever traveled with their catheters and bladder instillations? My IC Specialist said to "NOT" take my bladder instills with me b/c they would be funny about them, but to take plenty of pain meds.............well, i just realized that my pain meds will run out while we are in the middle of our trip......does anyone have any advice as to what i can do about this????? HELP! Meredith

contents: Hello, I have traveled to Spain with instillation meds and to many destinations within the US with instillation meds. I won't lie- it is a hassle to get them through security, but so far, I've been able to do it. My doctor wrote me a letter on his office letterhead stating exactly what my diagnosis is (Interstitial cystititis) and that I must do the treatments myself. He then named each item I need to carry with me, from the catheters & syringes, to the exact names of the medicines and the KY for lubrication of catheters. Even though the TSA website says you can have a private screening of your medical supplies going through security, I've never had that happen. What has helped is to go through the disabled travelers line and to lay everything out label side up in the plastic bin, with the doctor's letter on top. It does take extra time because usually the screeners call over their supervisors and sometimes the supervisors call for supervisors. I've never had anything confiscated, but because I'm always aware of the potential for it, I have a relative at home keep a box of my instillation supplies ready to fed ex to me the next day if I have to call for it. The last time I traveled cross country, I didn't want to hassle with it, so I fed-exed my instillation supplies to the friend I was visiting a few days before I arrived. She then checked to make sure everything was there, and it was a great relief not to have the extra wait and stress at the airport. I do the instillations 2x a day, so it was a lot to mail, but I'd probably do it again and I'm now budgeting the cost of mailing into my trip planning for my next trip. One thing about doing instillations out of country is the safety of the water supply. If you re-use catheters and syringes, by rinsing them out, you might want to not do that during your trip & use new equipment each time. I found in several places I traveled both inside & out of US, the water left a film inside my syringes and I wasn't sure about the safety of that. I'm not sure if your doctor meant airport security would be "funny" about your instillation supplies or if it would cause you problems with people traveling. I found in Spain that having my instillation supplies with me gave me greater credibility in the emergency room with a bladder infection. One doctor knew about IC, one didn't, but the sight of my catheters & syringes changed attitudes really fast & they both really wanted to help me. Of course, being fluent in Spanish & being able to explain IC to them also probably had something to do with that as well. Definitely talk with your doctor about getting extra pain meds for your trip. I'm a firm believer in bringing MORE than you think necessary because my last trip to Spain I got a really bad bladder infection and discovered that though the doctors there were very willing to help, they were not able to prescribe me darvocet, which is the only thing that consistently works for me without scary side effects. I ended up rationing out my pain meds & came home very sick and flaring. All that hassle aside, I do love to travel, in spite of IC, it's still great! I hope you have a fabulous time & please post about your trip afterwards

contents: I'd also google the TSA & look at their website for most recent travel & search restrictions. You can also call them and ask questions about disabled travelers and medical supplies, medicines. The airline usually isn't the one screening you for security, so they may not have the most up to date information, but it can't hurt to check in with them as well. Yes, your doctor can help you get your prescription filled early for travel. It's quite common, even for people without IC. And for anyone reading this who's planning longer flights, you can also talk with your pharmacist about how to time your medicines for maximum benefit while flying and for crossing multiple time zones. I hope you can get this bureaucratic stuff out of the way so you can just enjoy your trip!

contents: If you have refills you my just want to ask your pharmacist, take copies of your tickets with you, go ask now and say you are just want ing to know if it can be filled early in this situation or if you need you dr. to call in. I have been to D.R. take all you med supplies in a carry on and keep your hands on it. I hate to generlize a whole culture but they have very little and things like that are appealing to them. You can contact your airline most airlines do not count medical supplies as luggage. Enjoy your trip I am sure you will love it. I travel lots and have not had too many problems. But I don't carry the extra instill stuff. The Dr.'s note is a must. MG

contents: Hi everyone I haven't been on here for awhile I will be flying to NYC after thanksgiving. I havent flown on a plane since I got dx in 07'. Does anyone have any recomendations on flying for 6hrs to prevent a flare on the plane? Thank You

Ask for an aisle seat near restroom. It can help to show the flight attendant a restroom access card. Take along some IC safe snacks. I know you can't bring bottled water through security, but you can probably purchase some after you are in the secure area. You might want to check ahead of time about what's available. You'll want to have your medications with you in your carry on bag --- it's a good idea to have them in their prescription containers. There are some restrictions on liquids so if you take any, you'll want to check with the airline ahead of time. Finally, the best advice I have is to relax and enjoy your trip. I hope it's for something fun. Hugs, Donna

contents: Cushion Maybe a cushion would be helpful as well. Cindy-Lou

Originally posted by Garth949 Hi everyone I haven't been on here for awhile I will be flying to NYC after thanksgiving. I havent flown on a plane since I got dx in 07'. Does anyone have any recomendations on flying for 6hrs to prevent a flare on the plane? Thank You I've had to fly dozens of times since IC. Depending on the airline you can ask for special boarding where they let you pick seat that helps you. Southwest is great about this, they give you blue medical boarding sleeve and you board first after people in wheelchairs. Sitting was a big problem for me so I got a seat either near the front of plane where there was a divider wall to lean against or in back of plane where I could stand in flight attendant area - both areas are near the bathroom. Of course they will only let you stand when safe but it was a lifesaver to be able to stand most of the time and have wall to lean against. All it ever took for me was a doctors note on prescription pad saying I needed bathroom access and to be allowed to stand when safe due to medical condition. They never questioned it, barely looked at it.
signature: - Symptoms: started July 2007 New pain medication regimen has things under pretty good control for many weeks now. Exercise and eating healthy along with pain control have given me new life. Current Symptoms: pain in testicles, penis and general groin area. Other discomfort that is beyond pain, a maddening crazed feeling that defies description. New pain medication has most symptoms under good control. Frequency and urgency was a problem the first year or so but frequency is normal now. Diagnosed: Prostatitis first then IC by Cysto/Hydro Current Meds: Hydroxyzine 25mg 3 x day, Pyridium 200mg 3 x day (off 3-4 days on 3-4 days), Oxycodone, Norvasc (BP). Past medications and treatments: Protatitis and IC diet strict for 6 months, no change. Still watch diet although nothing seems to help or make worse. Have tried reverse, ate lemons to see if worse but no change at all. Meds tried - Amitriptyline, Detrol, Flomax, Cipro, Doxycycline. many other antibiotics, Uroxatrol, Vicoprofen, Elmiron (6 months at $320 mo nothing), Aloe, Cysprotek, Glucosamine, Marshmallow, various oils, tons more stuff to strange and numerous to recount. Hydrodistension for diagnostic was horrible for two weeks after I was sure I must be going to die because nothing could feel that way and let you live.

contents: I think I'm finally finishing up a 4 month long flare that began with a UTi last summer. I'm about 90 some per cent better but these last days of healing can be aaggravating as they are "up and down." I will have a couple of 100% perfect days and then the third day will be problematic with some urge or pinchy urethral spasms. I'm trying to keep an established pattern of activities and diet going while keeping stress to a minimum. I've only had 3 flares in 4 years so I'm still learning. As I recall my previous healings were very slow too.

Not sure where this post really goes but I thought I'ld place it here because it may be a way to help a flare. I am not sure what part the pillow actually plays in helping PFD, IC/ urethritis/ vulvodynia. I am not sure what it would actually help or if it is something that I would benenfit from. What are the indicators that you might benefit from this cushion. I do sit a lot at my desk as I am on the computer all day. When flaring I do feel pressure and experience lots of swelling after sitting all day. I feel less swelling and paian after sleeping and lying flat so I'm thinking that sitting upright might be adding to the pressure. I notice that I also tend to tighten my pelvis when I'm sitting during a flare. Maybe I am trying to stop the swelling....I can see my belly slowly get bigger and the pain steadily increases! Any thoughts> Thanks

contents: Hello there. I have PFD and am looking into treating it, but in the meantime, I've got frequency like crazy. I'll go the bathroom, then ten minutes later feel the need to go again. Usually I can hold it for two hours before allowing myself to go but still! And I've recently noticed that output is always way more than input. I typically only drink 2-3 cups of water or other beverage a day and I still have a TOTALLY FULL bladder after only 1.5-2 hours! It's madness! And it isn't dark yellow either, it's pretty pale and there's no smell or sign of infection. Any ideas or suggestions with dealing with this or what might be the cause of producing so much extra water?

That's interesting. I'm not sure what to tell you. I haven't had that problem. When I had the severe frequency issues i'd void drops which was very frustrating. Everything that I read says we should be drinking 6-8 glasses of water per day. I'd say you are on the way low end of this. Our bodies are mostly water so for your total health it would probably be best for you to consume more water. I know that can be tough with frequency. Hopefully someone else here can be of help to you.
signature: What helps Elmiron 200 mg bid Wellbutrin XL anxiety and depression Oxybutynin ER 10 mg Yoga tried and failed Cardura, Hytrin, Flomax, Prosed DS, Vesicare, Pyridium, Cystoprotek (caused GI problems), Lyrica, Pamelor "We can't wait until the storm is over. We need to learn to dance in the rain."

contents: What do ya'll do when you have an important business meeting or some other event where a flare could cost you your job or similar disaster? (Running in and out of room doubled over not an option.) I face this problem from time to time and I'm lost. Please help.

Can you take anything for the pain? Even something like Ativan which will calm you down both emotionally and might even simmer the bladder down a bit. I find it helps to the edge off a bit. Good luck......I know how you are feeling: grouphug:
signature: Mel IC, PFD, IBS, Allergies, Chronic Migraines, CVID, Fibromyalgia, CFS, Numerous Laporoscopies for Endometriosis and Adhesions, Hysterectomy 2002

contents: Originally posted by mela414 Can you take anything for the pain? Even something like Ativan which will calm you down both emotionally and might even simmer the bladder down a bit. I find it helps to the edge off a bit. Good luck......I know how you are feeling: grouphug: I really appreciate your response. Sadly, I can't tolerate pain meds generally speaking and ativan, diazepam, etc help with a flare but don't help prevent one. My flares can be incredibly random. (I stay on the diet and am very compliant with meds, PT, etc.) I'm wondering if pyridium 1/2 hour before important events might help. I hate the idea of taking a med I might not need but some situations really do need a safeguard. :/ Wish I knew what to do. My next appt with my uro is February and asking questions between appts isn't very effective.

If you have medications for pain, I suggest you talk with your doctor about taking one before an important meeting. Before I retired when I was in a long meeting, I took a seat near a door so I could quietly step out for a bathroom break. Donna

contents: Originally posted by ICNDonna If you have medications for pain, I suggest you talk with your doctor about taking one before an important meeting. Before I retired when I was in a long meeting, I took a seat near a door so I could quietly step out for a bathroom break. Donna When I think pain med I'm thinking vicodan, stuff like that. That stuffs not even an option for me. Do you classify pyridium as a pain med? (A little confused.) I'm not retired and sadly, there's nothing quiet about my flares. They come on like a mack truck. No way to be subtle. Wish there was. I've done a lot of bladder training and on a good day I go 2 hours between toilet breaks. During a flare it's every 5-10 minutes and intense bladder spasms. No way to be subtle. In the workplace, in this economy, this does not go well.

I would consider prydium a pain reliever --- it that helps avoid a flare, it could be a good option for you. If there are more than fifty employees where you work, they are required to provide "reasonable accommodation" for those with disabilities --- which includes IC. Donna

I'm so glad your docs cooked something up that pretty much works for you! Sadly instills are unlikely for me because of urethral issues. So far elevil and pyridium work the most effectively for me in emergencies. (Though I'd love it if there was something just plain better. Wouldn't we all?)
I have taken "preventative" pyridium before and it's really worked well for me - instances I've used it include long distance travels, before sex, etc.

contents: Yep, sure would!!! Are you using the Elavil only when you flare or are you taking it daily? I found it helpful the very first night I took it, but that it became even more helpful in reducing frequency & pain over the first two months of taking it nightly. Then it evened out & my doctor & I increased the dosage for more benefit. (Unfortunately, can't increase it any more than the 20mg I take now because I get ravenously hungry at 30mg. Darn!) Anyhow, if you're not taking it daily, you might find less flares over time if you did try to take it every night.... (To reduce the morning drowsiness I had initially, I'd take it at 7:30pm so I could wake up ok at 5:30am. Since I've been on it 7 years now, it doesn't matter, I can take it at bedtime no problem now.) Just a thought... I hope you find relief soon,

This site has been a great support to me the last few months. Thanks to everyone. I'm finally getting over a nasty flare that's come and gone over the last 4 months. I started doing pelvic exercises and walking,which have seemed to help in the past (had to stop until I could check out my chest pain). I also got really strict on the IC diet which I've never done. Except for juice and soda I couldn't tell that anything specific had flared me in the past. I also got brave enough to start seeing doctors about my ic again. I'm happy to say I had a much better experience this time. Things have changed in 12 years. Anyway here is my question. I haven't had a positive urine culture since my first visit to a doctor many moons ago and I'm not sure I really had a infection at that time. Since then my symptoms have all been pretty much the same so I always assumed they were ic related. (frequency, bladder pain, urithal pain, urgency) Some days are good and some are really bad. When I visited my ob/gyn to ask her about a possible prolapse she suggested checking for a uti so I said OK. My culture came back with a low count for strep b so I took the penicillin she prescribed. Finally started feeling better on the last day of the med. I know some of you have more experience with infections do you think I my flare was all from a UTI?? Resting seemed to improve my symptoms and I had no pain 1st thing in the mornings. Hoping I can learn for future reference. Thanks!!

My expirence is if you donèt start to feel better till last day of meds you may need more meds to knock out the bug completely. I found quite frequently I would take the meds just start to feel better and would be finished meds and a week later I would be back at square one. Now my Dr. always gets me to come back to see him the last day of my abs and then assess when my temp went down etc and decided if I need to take more abs. They cant dip at this point in time the meds will have knocked the count down enough not to show up on the test but still be there ready to grow back. You may want to talk to your Dr. or at the very least go back at first sign of getting sick. MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: I'm planning to follow up with a culture in a few days. My doctor wanted me to wait a week after finishing meds. I have continued to feel even better after finishing meds for 4 days. After frequecy of 15-25 a day I'm now down to only about 8. That's why I'm not really sure it was the med that made me feel so much better. Thanks for your input. Did your symptoms come and go when you had an infection? I was really surprised they found any bacteria.

Hey there! For me, a UTI could aggrivate my IC for days afterward until my bladder calmed back down. So, in my experience - they can go hand in hand, lol. The last UTI I had though, I thought for sure was a flare, but it was just a UTI - thank goodness! I was given some anti's and felt much better, lol. I swear ICers are the only ones who hope and pray for a UTI I too have had the experience of the round of anti's not being enough, or long enough to knock out the infection - had one come back and bite me because it wasn't knocked out completely and came back bigger and badder, lol. I had even done the follow-up urine recheck - but it was too close to the end of my antibiotic course, so it came back fine, even though I didn't feel fine. I just chalked it up to it aggrivating my IC, until a week later when the backache and fever set in, yuck. It came back so severe that my doc even offered to stick me in the hospital to get round the clock IV antibiotics, geesh. (Never mind had they listened to me in the first place it wouldn't have happened - I knew something was still going on when I called them back after 2 days of being off the antibiotics, lol) I opted for a shot to get things kicked off then an Rx, lol. I am glad though that you seem to be feeling better!!!!

signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Originally posted by traceann The last UTI I had though, I thought for sure was a flare, but it was just a UTI - thank goodness! I was given some anti's and felt much better, lol. I swear ICers are the only ones who hope and pray for a UTI I started having what I thought was a flare a couple of days ago, which puzzled me because I hadn't eaten/drank/done anything different. Today I woke up with frequency issues, which I don't normally have. So, I thought, maybe this is a UTI and went to see my family doc. I was expecting him to say, you're fine, but he said that my white blood cell count was elevated. Luckily, he was very sympathetic, saying, "When you have IC it's sometimes hard to know if you're having a flare or a UTI, so you did the right thing." I did mention reading that sometimes a flare will elevate the white cell count, so he said he'd have my urine cultured and call me back if it was negative for bacteria so I could stop the antibiotics. I guess in the meantime I'll take them (and I had him write me a Diflucan, too, because I know what's coming next!) But yeah, part of me is glad it might be a UTI and not a mysterious flare!

contents: Yes what is approved in one country is not the same in the other. For example AZO or the stuff it actually is prydrium (sp) you can bye OTC in the US and now it is band in Canada, or so I have heard people on thsi site say. I buy the AZO when I am in the USA the RX strength cause's me to be nauseated and gives me retention, the rx ones in Canada can't be broken in have to get a lower dose, they are coated to pass the stomach and not cause the upset. So for me it is just much easier to buy the OTC ones and take the lower dose. I only take it once in a while, if I take it everyday even just one OTC it will cause me retention. But if I have a long car ride I will take one because I can go longer between pit stops, and my bladder is happier with it. Have a good day MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: I am so confused about UTIs. Up until I was diagnoised with IC in 2008 I only had two UTIs in my life. However, the second one did me in and low and behold I developed IC. Last month I had my third UTI and Dr. Evans prescribed meds. I felt much better and thought it was gone. Now here I am with possibly another one. When I pee it burns and in between trips to the potty it kinda itches like a yeast infection. My bladder seems calm and I don't have any pain in my belly. So could this be just a common symptom of vulodynia or is the UTI back? Any advice? I don't think I really understand what's what anymore. There seems to be something new or weird every day. Figuring it all out is frustrating. I hate to call the URO if I don't need meds.
I am not a dr. but I would lean towards maybe a yeast infection, but you should consult a dr. about any new symptoms. MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Originally posted by Mothergoose I am not a dr. but I would lean towards maybe a yeast infection, but you should consult a dr. about any new symptoms. MG I agree. I think you can get yeast in the tissues surrounding your urethra, which might explain the symptoms you're having. And if I've been on antibiotics for a while, that's always my first suspicion. I know it's a pain to keep going back to the doctor, but it might be a relatively simple problem and if you fix it, you may feel a lot better.

contents: Thanks for the advice. I am fortunate to have good insurance and I can afford to go to the drs office, but I feel like his time is so valuable to other patients, I hate to call about every little thing. But I am very fearful to try something over the counter. I am just now beginning to feel better after having a flare that lasted since March. My body and my spirit is vulnerable. My family has been supportive and loving. Yet, it is so nice to have people like yourselves who understand what I am going thru. And the advice comes from actual experiences. I am trully blessed and this site is awesome.

Not a UTI after all My doctor's office called this morning - the urine culture is negative for bacteria, so they told me to stop the macrobid. Sometimes I just get so frustrated. I do everything I can to learn about IC and what I can do to help myself feel better. I try all kinds of medications and supplements, plus alternative treatments. I've been fairly strict on the diet. I almost never try anything off the "bladder friendly" column, and I certainly haven't had even the tiniest bit of coffee, soda, alcohol, tomatoes, etc. since February. I sometimes feel like it's my fault that I still get flares. It's like there's something I'm not doing right, I'm not trying hard enough. I have days where I feel like I'll never figure it out. I know in my head that I've only been diagnosed for four months now, and that improvement comes gradually for many. I know that I'm being impatient. It's just hard not to get discouraged sometimes.

contents: Hang in there, Wagamama. Sounds like you're doing all that you can do for now. IC and it's accompanying problems are very fickled in nature. Just when you think you've found an answer, it switches gears. Many of us have had remissions from time to time that give us hope. You're at the best place for continued ed concerning the ins and out of IC. And I do believe sharing here at the IC forum has helped me understand what I'm going thru. One of the most valuable bits of information I've learned is not every drug, suppplement or herbal remedy works for everyone. And trust me, all of us are impatient and anxious when we're in pain. So don't beat yourself up.

You probalby are not doing anything wrong, it is just your body is different, I have found that it is such a trail and error game, for example I can take D-mannose in am but not in pm go figure, I can take OTC AZO but not RX of the same stuff but stronger. I can get away with a bit of some food but not if I have eaten someother food in the last few days. I don't know to exaplain it to you to figure these things out just that it took a long time and a lot of aha moments when it would hit me something worked or didn't work. I can probiotics if they are quite pure and I take them with food in the orning only who know why. You might want to keep good records of what you eat and do over a peroid of many weeks and see if you can pick out a patteren. Then of course there is always foods on the okay list that may not be okay with you. It has only been 4 months, it has taken me years but i have learn't more about IC in the past year and a half from this site than all the years before it put together. good luck MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: "That's just IC" I went today and sure enough I had a full blown yeast infection and bladder infection. It is the second round since the middle of June. As I was leaving with a handful of prescriptions, I ask, "what am I doing wrong"? She very sympathically said with a slight grin on her face, "that's just IC". As I sat at the pharmacy waiting for my medicine, I closed my eyes and prayed that one day we are all free of suffering the daily reminders of "that's just IC".

update Saw my urologist after taking penicillin and still getting a positive test back for strep B. He says I don't have a infection and doesn't think I had one to begin with. He checked my urine under a microscope personally to look for bacteria and said it was clear. I thought my symptoms were just IC too. I've been feeling great for a month now with only an occasional mild flare. I've been walking for over a month now and planning to start running again tomorrow. I'm still learning about how to deal with doctors and IC, but I'm thankful for my Lord and Savior who has always been there to help me through the hard times. Thanks, Vicki

Amen to that! I am learning everyday too. IC life is so fickled. At least we are not alone in this journey and can help one another cope. And along the way we share valuable information that might help us learn more about our illness.
I went swimming yesterday and it seems to have triggered a flare. Does this happen to anyone else? Does this mean I should not swim?
signature: Check me out on facebook: http://www.facebook.com/glory.ic Diagnosed in early 7-2008 w/ IC: glomerations, mucosal cracking, & crystal embedding in the trigone. First cystoscopy & hydrodistention 7-2008 Fervent prayer to God-I'm seeking divine healing on God's time table-it hasn't happened yet but I trust in Him for deliverance! Tried: Hydroxyzine HCL 50mg-I was a zombie! ,Took 300 mg Elmiron daily (over a year later), Instills, Amitryptiline, Cymbalta, Acupuncture, Herbs & marshmallow root tea, Hydrocodone than oxycodone for pain, Tramadol, Soma, Phenazopyridine (pyridium), MSM gel Still taking: Gabapentin (3 pills each night-300mg each), Melatonin (natural supplement) The latest, trying to cut out wheat & sugar to see if it helps ( I am desperate to find something-other than meds)! I just got off of a oxycodone habit that I originally was using to try & kill pain to function. I believe I had Opioid-induced hyperalgesia! My pain is not much worse giving up the pain meds after taking them for 4.5 years...it's about the same! It was a vicious cycle! IC runs in my family-My aunt has the same condition. The women on my maternal side have frequency issues. I have had frequency for many years. The pain started in December 2005 after I was in a car accident. Other : Hip disease (legg perthes as a child-resulting in a damaged femoral head), I had total hip replacement on my right hip in April 2010 at the age of 34. Had a hysterectomy in October 2010 due to pelvic pain & an unusual uterus.

contents: We have an above ground pool and use Baquacil instead of clorine to keep it clean. Some ICers can swim in a clorinated pool --- and some put vaseline on the perineal area to help protect it --- and it's not good to sit around in a wet swim suit. I hope you feel better quickly. Warm hugs, Donna

contents: Prelief is over the counter. It's not really a treatment for IC, but it helps by neutralizing the acids in foods. It doesn't allow me to eat anything I want, but if I'm eating away from home and am not certain the foods are IC safe, swallowing two Prelief just before the first bite of food will help avoid a flare for me. I buy mine from a local grocery store. It's with the antacids. If you scroll to the top of this page and click on "Prelief" you can find out where it's available in your area. Or you can order it on line. Prelief has been a real benefit for me and I won't be without it. Donna
